items.caption_text:
To every young aviator dreaming of the skies ✈️

This journey isn’t just about flying aircraft — it’s about discipline, resilience, and showing up even on the days you doubt yourself. There will be setbacks, long nights, tough checks, and moments where you question everything… but that’s exactly what shapes you into a pilot.

Stay consistent. Stay curious. Keep learning — not just about flying, but about yourself.

The view from the @boeing cockpit isn’t given, it’s earned. And trust me, it’s worth every bit of the grind.

Happy World Pilot’s Day 🤍See you in the skies. 🌍✨

biography:
🌱 We exist to help you grow.
🌻 Our seeds: @botanical_interests

items.caption_text:
BASIL GROWING HACK - Most people let basil grow straight up, eventually wondering why it looks tall, spindly, and exhausted. However, there is a simple trick to transform your harvest by cutting just above the nodes. Look for the little crossroads on the stem where tiny leaves are waiting to sprout, because that one small snip changes the entire biological trajectory of the plant by shifting its focus from height to health.

When you prune above a node, the plant does more than just grow back. It diverts its energy into two new branches from that exact point, effectively turning one stem into two. If you repeat this process on those new stems, you suddenly have four. Through this exponential growth, you stop the plant from stretching vertically and instead force it to build a wide and sturdy foundation.

Give it a few months and the difference is night and day. Unpruned basil rushes to flower and seed as it becomes sparse and woody, while pruned basil transforms into a dense bush with massive harvests. It is the same plant and the same soil, but your decisions dictate the outcome. By guiding the growth rather than just watching it, you turn a tired herb into a lush kitchen powerhouse.

items.caption_text:
Comment “homestead” to see us build a homestead from scratch!
STARTING A HOMESTEAD - My homestead is proof that even a small piece of land can be engineered into a food-producing system that runs on intention. Raised beds keep everything organized and easy to maintain, rainwater capture turns every roof into usable irrigation, and a backyard test garden constantly filters in what actually performs. Add chickens cycling nutrients back into the soil and an in-ground section for high-yield crops, and you get a setup that quietly compounds season after season.

It’s not about having more space, it’s about making every square foot work together. Each part of the system feeds the next, turning waste into input and effort into long-term return. That’s how a third-acre ends up producing hundreds of pounds of food when every piece has a purpose.

And if you want to see that same mindset applied from the ground up, we started a brand new homestead from scratch in Maine in partnership with @homedepot. Follow along as we build it piece by piece and show exactly how a raw space turns into a working food system. #ad

items.caption_text:
BANANA LEAF POT - Most people see a banana leaf pot and think it’s just a pretty old-school trick, but it actually works like giving a seedling a house it never has to move out of. With plastic pots, it’s like packing a plant into a box and then ripping it open at the worst moment, breaking tiny root hairs that are doing all the real work. With banana leaves, you just plant the whole thing straight into the soil and the roots keep growing like nothing ever changed.

What’s really happening is the leaf slowly turns into soil, like the pot is dissolving back into the garden and becoming part of it. It feeds the microbes that feed your plant, like turning the container into a slow meal right at the roots. And because it breathes instead of trapping heat and water like plastic, it acts more like a woven basket than a sealed cup, letting air move through so the roots stay healthy instead of suffocating. Nature’s smartest designs are usually the simplest materials.

items.caption_text:
CHICKEN COOP HACK - Most people think cleaning more = healthier coop, but the deep litter method flips that idea. Instead of removing waste, you’re building a living system. Each layer of manure + bedding becomes fuel for microbes. Think of it like a forest floor… nothing gets “cleaned,” yet nothing smells. That’s because beneficial microbes are actively breaking waste down in real time.

The real shift is thinking in ratios, not mess. Chicken manure is nitrogen-heavy, wet, and quick to release ammonia. Add a carbon-rich material like @grubblyfarms hemp bedding, and suddenly you’ve got balance. The bedding acts like a sponge holding moisture while also feeding microbes with carbon. When that balance is right, microbes outcompete odor-causing processes and transform raw waste into stable organic matter.

Maintenance is simple but strategic. Every few weeks, add a fresh layer to keep that carbon-to-nitrogen ratio in check. If you smell ammonia, that’s your signal the system is out of balance add more dry bedding and improve airflow. Over time, the coop becomes self-sanitizing as microbial populations explode. After about six months, you’ve got partially composted material ready to finish in a pile and eventually feed your garden soil. #ad

items.caption_text:
DRAGON FRUIT PRUNING - When your plant turns into a jungle, more growth isn’t always better. Every stem can flower, but the plant can’t support an unlimited number of fruits at once. It’s like a factory with too many orders and not enough workers. You end up with smaller fruit, slower ripening, and stressed plants instead of a clean, productive harvest.

So what’s actually happening when you make these cuts? Dragon fruit allocates energy through its stems, and overcrowding spreads those resources thin. By thinning stems so they don’t touch, you improve light exposure and airflow, which boosts photosynthesis and reduces disease pressure. Upright, top-heavy growth is also structurally weak, like stacking weight on a narrow tower, so removing those stems prevents breakage and redirects energy into stronger, fruiting arms.

And nothing goes to waste. Those cuttings are basically clones, ready to root and grow into new plants, often worth around $12 each. Pruning isn’t just cleanup, it’s resource management. You’re telling the plant where to invest its energy, turning wild growth into focused production and stronger, more reliable harvests.

items.caption_text:
FAST FOOD FERTILIZER - What happens if you “fertilize” tomatoes with Taco Bell? Three identical plants, three Cheesy Gordita Crunches buried three different ways: whole, chopped, and minced. The idea was simple… more surface area = faster breakdown = more available nutrients. Kind of like turning a log into wood chips vs leaving it whole. Same material, totally different speed of decomposition.

Here’s where the science kicks in. Plants don’t eat food scraps directly, they rely on microbes to break organic matter down into forms roots can absorb. When you bury something dense and greasy, you’re basically asking soil microbes to work overtime. The minced taco had more surface area, so microbes could access it faster, while the whole taco decomposed slowly, tying up nitrogen in the process. That early “boost” you see in growth? Often just a temporary flush, not sustained nutrition.

The result? The control plant, with nothing buried underneath, pulled 15 pounds of tomatoes, nearly double the taco-fed plants. Why? Because nutrients only count when they’re fully broken down and bioavailable. Until then, they can actually compete with your plant for resources. Lesson here: compost first, feed later. Your tomatoes don’t want fast food… they want finished soil.

items.caption_text:
LOGS IN RAISED BED - Two identical raised beds, one big question: does Hugelkultur actually outperform a standard soil-filled bed? @jacquesinthegarden set up a clean test with the same soil and setup, but one bed swaps the bottom third for logs and wood chips, hauled in with @gorillamadeproducts’ Gorilla Cart. Think of it like two houses with the same paint job, but completely different foundations underneath.

Here’s the science: as that buried wood breaks down, microbes and fungi move in and build underground networks that cycle nutrients and improve soil structure. It acts like a slow-release sponge or even a buried pantry, holding water and feeding plants over time. That’s why Hugelkultur beds often stay moist longer and can grow more resilient plants, plus you save about 30 percent on soil upfront.

But nothing in nature is static. As the wood decomposes, it shrinks like a settling mattress, and the whole bed sinks with it. That means you’ll keep adding soil to top it off, trading early savings for long-term maintenance. Jacques is watching it closely, but the big takeaway is this: you’re not just building a bed, you’re setting up a living system that changes over time.

raw.caption:
LOGS IN RAISED BED - Two identical raised beds, one big question: does Hugelkultur actually outperform a standard soil-filled bed? @jacquesinthegarden set up a clean test with the same soil and setup, but one bed swaps the bottom third for logs and wood chips, hauled in with @gorillamadeproducts’ Gorilla Cart. Think of it like two houses with the same paint job, but completely different foundations underneath.

Here’s the science: as that buried wood breaks down, microbes and fungi move in and build underground networks that cycle nutrients and improve soil structure. It acts like a slow-release sponge or even a buried pantry, holding water and feeding plants over time. That’s why Hugelkultur beds often stay moist longer and can grow more resilient plants, plus you save about 30 percent on soil upfront.

But nothing in nature is static. As the wood decomposes, it shrinks like a settling mattress, and the whole bed sinks with it. That means you’ll keep adding soil to top it off, trading early savings for long-term maintenance. Jacques is watching it closely, but the big takeaway is this: you’re not just building a bed, you’re setting up a living system that changes over time.

items.caption_text:
Comment “FIG” and I'll send you the link to get your very own
PRUNING FIG TREES - Pruning isn’t just about making things look tidy, you’re actually telling the plant exactly where to put its energy. Think of your tree like a factory with a limited power supply. Figs only grow on new wood, so last year’s branches are basically retired workers. When you cut the tree back, you flip a switch that triggers a flush of fresh growth. That new wood is exactly where your fruit will form. If you don’t prune, you will end up with a giant bush of leaves but very little fruit to show for it.

Timing matters just as much as the cuts. Pruning when the tree is dormant and the leaves have dropped works with the plant’s natural rhythm. The tree has all its energy tucked away in the roots for winter. When spring hits, it pushes that power into the remaining buds. By cutting branches back hard, you are concentrating all that stored energy into just a few spots. This leads to much stronger shoots and way more fruit.

As you shape the tree, you want to clear out any awkward or crossing branches to keep the air moving. Taking out about 30 percent of the tree might feel scary, but it creates a perfect balance. The best part is that those cuttings aren’t trash. You can root them to start brand new fig trees for free. With a few smart cuts, you aren’t just boosting this season’s harvest, you are setting up future ones too.

items.caption_text:
GROWING TOMATO IN CONTAINERS - Planting tomatoes in containers isn’t just about saving space. It’s about being the master of your plant’s universe. Think of a 15 gallon pot like a big, open floor plan for a house. It gives the roots plenty of room to stretch out and find food and water. In a small pot, the roots hit the walls too fast and feel cramped, which tells the plant to slow down growth. Pair that space with a quality mix like @foxfarmsoilandfertilizer and you’re setting up a root zone that’s built to perform from day one.

When it’s time to move your plant into its permanent home, you want a hole that fits like a glove. This keeps the roots happy and prevents stress. Dropping some fertilizer right into that hole is like giving your plant a packed lunch exactly where it needs it, helping it settle in fast. I also like to trim off those bottom leaves so the plant focuses its energy on upward growth instead of wasting it near the soil surface.

Once the plant is in, you have to keep the environment stable. Mulch acts like a protective layer to keep the soil cool and hold onto moisture. You want to avoid rollercoaster watering where the soil swings from dry to soaked, because that is how you end up with cracked fruit or blossom end rot. Every deep watering moves nutrients through the soil, so staying consistent with a liquid feed keeps growth steady and strong.

items.caption_text:
GARLIC GROWING EXPERIMENT - Every garlic clove is essentially a stored energy package, and how that energy is used determines the quality of the bulb you harvest. When garlic is planted too deep, such as 6 inches, it exhausts its energy simply trying to reach the surface. This leaves less fuel available for bulb development, which results in smaller yields and a more difficult harvest.

Clove size plays a critical role in this process. Larger cloves are packed with more stored carbohydrates, which fuel stronger early growth and larger leaves. This leads to bigger bulbs, creating a direct correlation between input and output. Interestingly, planting a whole garlic head revealed that while multiple cloves growing together will compete for resources, they can still produce a cluster of solid bulbs in a single spot.

The largest bulbs resulted from giving the garlic the best possible start by soaking cloves in a mixture of fertilizer and hydrogen peroxide before planting them at a standard depth. The peroxide helps reduce disease pressure and increases oxygen levels around the clove, while the nutrients kickstart root development. When a plant establishes strong roots early, it can focus its energy where it matters most: growing large, healthy bulbs underground.

items.caption_text:
TRANSPLANTING MADE EASY - Transplanting into a bigger container is kind of like moving a kid out of a cramped room into a space where they can finally stretch out. With @foxfarmsoilandfertilizer, the official soil of Epic Gardening, you’re not just giving plants more room, you’re setting them up with a rich environment to actually grow into. When roots stay too long in a small pot, they start circling like they’re stuck in a loop, which makes it harder for them to take in water and nutrients.

A simple trick makes that move a lot smoother. Before you take the plant out, press the small pot into the new soil to create a perfect mold. Think of it like making a custom seat that fits exactly right. When the hole matches the root ball, there are no awkward gaps where air can dry things out, and the plant settles in at just the right depth without wobbling or sinking.

When you pop the plant out, gently loosen the roots if they’re all tangled up. It’s like helping them find a new direction so they don’t keep going in circles. Set it into its new home, water it in, and you’ve just made the transition easier on the plant. A small step in prep but it sets the plant up to take off instead of stall. 🌱

items.caption_text:
Comment 'UP' to get your hands on zucchini seeds!
GROWING ZUCCHINI VERTICALLY - With a single stake and a bit of twine, you can train your zucchini to grow upward instead of sprawling across the soil. As the plant grows, tie it up every few inches to keep it supported and heading skyward, turning what’s usually a space-hog into a compact, manageable producer. 

The real advantage comes down to airflow and light. When zucchini leaves are lifted off the ground and spaced out vertically, moisture dries faster and air circulates more freely around the plant. That makes it much harder for fungal issues like powdery mildew to take hold. At the same time, better light exposure improves photosynthesis, helping the plant put out stronger growth and more consistent fruit. 

As it climbs, removing the lower leaves helps even more. You reduce the risk of soil-borne disease while redirecting energy toward fruit production instead of excess foliage. The result is a healthier plant, easier harvesting, and no more lost zucchinis hiding under a jungle of leaves. Chaos solved.

items.caption_text:
CARROT GERMINATION EXPERIMENT - I tested every carrot germination trick I could find… and the “boring” method won.

Standard sowing: 19/20. This one just works. The seeds had consistent moisture, direct soil contact, and enough airflow. No interference and no extra steps, just the conditions carrots actually need.

Radish companion: 15/20. Pretty solid. The radishes helped break up the soil as they grew and acted as a natural row marker, which made it easier to keep watering consistent without overdoing it.

Overnight soak: 15/20. Soaking helped kickstart germination by hydrating the seed faster, but once planted, some likely struggled from reduced oxygen if the soil stayed too wet.

Pre-sprouting: 11/20. This sounds smart, but carrots really don’t like root disturbance. That first tiny taproot forms quickly, and moving it can damage it or slow it down.

Cardboard cover: 10/20. It held moisture in, which is helpful, but it also limited airflow. Seeds don’t just need water, they need oxygen too, especially early on.

Cornstarch gel: 2/20. Way too much moisture sitting around the seed. It basically created the perfect environment for mold, and most of the seeds rotted before they could sprout.

Seed tape: 0/20. Same issue, just worse. The material held onto moisture for too long, cutting off airflow and leading to complete rot.

Now the methods that failed all had one thing in common: excess moisture without airflow. So the takeaway is simple. Carrot seeds don’t need complicated tricks. They need three things: steady moisture, good soil contact, and enough oxygen to breathe.

items.caption_text:
PEST PROOFING RAISED BEDS - Protecting a raised bed works best when you think in layers, because each type of pest behaves differently. Burrowing pests move through soil, weeds rely on light to sprout, and flying insects look for direct access to leaves. So instead of one fix, you stack barriers that interrupt each of those behaviors and close off entry points from below and above.

Layer 1 is landscape fabric, placed at the very bottom. This works by blocking sunlight, which weed seeds need to germinate, keeping them dormant underneath your bed. Layer 2 is 1/2 inch hardware cloth, which acts as a physical barrier that burrowing pests like gophers, voles, and moles cannot squeeze through, while still allowing water and roots to pass. Layer 3 is cardboard, which adds a temporary barrier that blocks light and space, then slowly breaks down into organic matter, feeding soil microbes and improving structure over time.

On top, the frame and frost cloth protect from above. The frame keeps the cover stable and sealed, while the frost cloth acts like a filter, letting sunlight, air, and water through but blocking flying pests from reaching your plants. When all layers work together, you get a system that uses simple physics and biology to protect your crops, without relying on constant intervention.

items.caption_text:
Reply with “peppers” in the comments and I will send you where I get my seeds 🌶️
TOPPING PEPPERS — Topping or pruning the main stem of a pepper plant does more than just shape it. It actually changes how the plant grows. When you remove the top of the main stem, the plant redirects energy from vertical growth into lateral branches, giving it more sites for flowers and eventually fruit.

At first, this can make the plant look weaker or slower. In my experiment, the unpruned plant grew taller and set fruit earlier because it focused all its energy on growing up. The topped plant seemed delayed at first, but by branching out from the base and middle nodes, it eventually created far more flowering sites.

After about four months, the results were clear. The topped jalapeño produced 37 peppers, nearly three times more than the unpruned plant’s 15. This shows how a simple early prune can trade quick vertical growth for a bigger, more productive harvest.

⚠️ Caveat: Don’t prune if you have a short growing season, as topping slows the plant’s development too much. For large varieties like bell peppers, topping is not recommended because the yield increase isn’t significant enough to be worth the effort.

items.caption_text:
Comment “CHAMOMILE” and I’ll let you in on my seed source 🌱
CHAMOMILE HARVEST - Chamomile is kind of like that low maintenance friend who still shows up every time. Once it settles in, it drops its own seeds and comes back on its own, so you are not restarting from scratch each season. It is an easy rhythm to work with, especially if you want something dependable that fits into almost any garden space.

One of my favorite parts is harvest time. Instead of picking flower by flower, I use a simple chamomile booper to gently scoop blooms in batches, making the process faster while keeping the plant productive. The scent is always noticeable too, light and sweet, almost like green apple candy. That aroma comes from natural compounds the plant produces, which not only make it enjoyable for us but also help attract beneficial insects into the garden.

Chamomile does more than just look nice or smell good. Those small flowers act like landing spots for pollinators, bringing more activity into your space and supporting nearby plants. Once harvested, the blooms can be dried and used for tea, preserving those same natural compounds. It is the kind of plant that gives back at every stage, easy to grow, helpful to the garden, and something you can enjoy long after harvest.

items.caption_text:
GROWING POTATOES IN BUCKETS — Did you know you can influence how productive your potato plant will be before it even hits the soil? Prep your seed potatoes by “chitting” them and leaving only two eyes to sprout. It might feel counterintuitive, but this is all about energy allocation. Instead of spreading resources across a bunch of weak shoots, the plant channels its stored starches into two strong sprouts that can support better tuber development later on.

For the soil, keep it light and rich with a 50 50 mix of peat moss and compost. This balance gives you both moisture retention and airflow, which is key because potatoes form underground stems called stolons that need loose soil to expand. As you build your bucket, think in layers. Fill a seven gallon bucket one third at a time, placing two seed potatoes per layer. This mimics hilling by encouraging more sites along the buried stems where new potatoes can form.

At each layer, mix in a bit of blood meal, fish meal, and an all purpose fertilizer to supply steady nutrients as the plant grows. Once filled, top it with straw to lock in moisture and regulate temperature, then water it in well. When the plants flower and eventually die back, that is your signal that tuber formation is complete. From just a few seed potatoes, you can end up with a full bucket harvest of 15 to 20 potatoes, all from a setup that fits in a small space.

A small bucket, a few seed potatoes, and a little strategy can go a long way 🥔🌱

items.caption_text:
Comment “tomato” to get our hand-picked seed varieties perfect for beginner growers 🌱

WHEN TO PICK TOMATOES - We all love the idea of a true “vine-ripened” tomato, slowly turning red under the sun until it’s perfectly ready to pick. But here’s where the plant science gets interesting. There’s a point called the breaker stage, when a tomato hits about 50% of its final color, and at that moment, it’s already received everything it needs from the plant in terms of sugars, nutrients, and overall development.

From there, the ripening process is driven internally. Ethylene production kicks in, triggering the chemical changes that develop sweetness, aroma, and that classic tomato flavor. So whether it finishes ripening on the vine or on your kitchen counter, the end result is nearly identical, because the flavor compounds are already set in motion.

Harvesting at the breaker stage just means you’re working with the plant instead of against it. You reduce the risk of pests, splitting after heavy rain, or losing fruit right before it peaks. Turns out, the smartest “vine-ripened” tomato might just be the one you bring in a little early 🍅

items.caption_text:
Grow these edible beauties by commenting “nasty” below!
GROWING NASTURTIUMS - Nasturtiums are one of those plants that quietly earn their place in the garden. Every part of the plant is edible including the leaves, flowers, stems, and seeds, all with a naturally peppery flavor that comes from mustard oils, the same compounds found in crops like arugula and radishes. Toss the flowers into salads, use the leaves for a little bite, or pickle the seeds for a surprisingly good substitute for capers.

They are also incredibly easy to grow. Nasturtiums actually thrive in poorer soils because excess nitrogen pushes leafy growth instead of flowers, which is why they often perform better where other crops struggle. Plant them once and they frequently reseed themselves, meaning a single planting can quietly return year after year with almost no effort.

Where they really shine is along the edges of a raised bed. Their bright flowers and chemical signals tend to attract pests like aphids, pulling pressure away from your vegetables and acting as a natural trap crop. Plant them on the borders and you are not just adding color, you are creating a living buffer that helps protect your harvest.

And the best part? A plant that feeds the soil, feeds the pollinators, feeds you… and still looks good doing it. Not a bad deal for something that started as a simple border flower.

Comment “CARROT” to check out our full carrot seed collection!

CARROT GERMINATION TRICK - Carrot seeds have one major weakness during germination: they dry out fast. Because the seeds are so small, they’re usually planted very shallow, which means the top layer of soil can lose moisture quickly to sun and wind. Pressing a stick into loose soil to create a shallow trench helps concentrate moisture in that small channel and improves seed-to-soil contact, both of which are key signals that trigger germination.

Watering the trench before sowing is another small step that makes a big difference. When the soil is already evenly moist, the seed coat can begin absorbing water immediately. This process, called imbibition, activates the enzymes inside the seed that kickstart germination. If dry soil is watered after sowing, the water can sometimes shift the tiny seeds around or leave them unevenly hydrated.

Placing a stick directly over the row acts like a simple micro-moisture barrier. It lightly shades the soil surface and slows evaporation right where the seeds are sitting. Because carrot seeds are planted so shallow, even a short dry spell can interrupt germination. Keeping that top layer consistently moist gives the seeds the stable conditions they need to sprout. Once the first seedlings begin to emerge, remove the stick and let them grow freely. A small adjustment like this can be the difference between scattered sprouts and a clean, evenly filled row of carrots. 🥕

items.caption_text:
What is “WORKABLE SOIL”? Shannie from @botanical_interests is here to break it down - After winter, garden soil usually isn’t ready to plant in right away. Freezing temperatures and snowmelt often leave the soil compacted and waterlogged, which creates that sticky, muddy texture gardeners dread. What we’re really waiting for is soil that’s “workable” or “friable,” meaning it has dried enough to become loose, crumbly, and easy for roots to grow through.

A quick way to check is with a simple soil test. Dig about 6 to 8 inches down, grab a handful of soil, and squeeze it into a ball. Then toss that ball about 6 inches into the air and catch it in your palm. It might feel a little silly, but this test gives you a fast read on whether your soil is ready to work.

Here’s how to interpret the results. If the ball stays completely intact, the soil is still too wet and needs more time. If it breaks into two or three chunks, you’re getting close, but it likely needs a few more days to dry. But if the ball crumbles apart when it lands, that’s the sweet spot. Your soil is friable and ready for planting. 🌱

items.caption_text:
Want this amazing tree for your garden? Comment LOQUAT and I’ll send you the link to grab one 🌳

LOQUAT TREE BENEFITS - Loquat is one of those fruit trees that doesn’t get talked about enough. It’s incredibly low-maintenance, grows well in many climates, and once it’s established, it can produce a surprising amount of fruit each season. If you’re looking for a productive tree that doesn’t demand much attention, this one is worth considering.

One thing I always recommend is starting with a grafted loquat tree instead of growing from seed. Seed-grown trees often produce fruit that’s inconsistent or not very flavorful. A grafted variety gives you a much better shot at those sweet-tangy loquats people love. Just give the tree plenty of sunlight and plant it in well-draining soil, because loquats really don’t like having “wet feet.”

Another interesting side of this plant is how useful it’s been beyond the garden. Loquat leaves have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, often brewed into tea to support digestion and respiratory health. Between the fruit for jams and fresh eating and the leaves for tea, it’s one of those trees that almost feels like a small living pharmacy in the backyard.

items.caption_text:
GROWING WASABI - Wasabi might be one of the most challenging plants I’ve ever tried to grow. I’ll be honest, I’ve already failed twice. This third attempt is me hoping I finally get it right. It’s a notoriously tricky crop, and there’s a reason most people never try growing it at home.

In the wild, wasabi grows along cold, misty mountain streams where the temperature stays cool and the air stays humid. Trying to recreate that environment in the garden is tough. On top of that, the plant itself is surprisingly fragile. The stems and leaves snap easily, so even handling it takes a little extra care.

And patience is a huge part of the process. Wasabi can take up to three years before the rhizome is ready to harvest. When you think about how difficult it is to grow and how long it takes, it makes sense why real, fresh wasabi can sell for over $250 per kilogram. If I can keep this one alive, it’ll definitely be worth the wait.

Fun fact: the spicy kick of real wasabi comes from compounds called isothiocyanates, which activate receptors in your nose rather than your tongue. That’s why the heat feels sharp but fades quickly compared to chili peppers!

Swipe for behind-the-scenes 🩵 new stop-motion work for LEGO Bluey.

Exploring fun and rhythmic movement is my favorite thing to do, especially when mixing characters and object physics. A big experiment on this one was switching lenses while shooting, and then blending them with post-production scaling. I think it worked really well! Keeps the single angle simplicity but adds a dynamic quality to the camera.

items.caption_text:
One of my wishes for this year was to spend more time at home in the Carpathians. So far, I’ve not exactly succeeded at this, in between expedition Sahara and the book tour - in fact, I write this post from Scotland.

It’s getting increasingly difficult for me to be away from home, and yet absence from home is one of the key demands of my job.

In particular, I end up missing the serenity of my land, and of course my horse, Shaman. He’s in the best of hands: he’s stabled at my dear friends’ property, hanging out with his herd. (Which he loves: horses are very different from dogs and in general would much rather chill with their horse friends than with us humans).

When I was home this year, I spent all my time among the horses - training, learning - and absorbing as much of the mountain as possible.

There’s something so grounding in being among horses. In falling asleep next to a crackling fire.

When the season gets warmer, once the book is out in the world, and my book tour complete, I’ll come back home and bury myself in my rugged little world once more.

In the meantime, I’ll miss it just a little every day.

items.caption_text:
Did you believe in the witches who lived alone in the woods? 🧙‍♀️

The image of the witch in the woods was one that haunted my childhood. I was so obsessed with it that I once even wrote a college dissertation about the persona of the witch in the fairytales of the Brothers Grimm.

Of course, the image of the witch as we know, it is a caricature – they took an independent woman who lives alone and answers to no one, and vilified her. Turned her into a monster.

Her freedom turned into horror, her independence turned into something to be feared.

Because, according to the mores of those times, the only way to live as a woman was to birth babies, take care of the household and serve your husband. (Has much changed?)

I reckon it’s time to reclaim this woman’s freedom and clear her image.

Long live the witch in the woods!

Despite the many challenges that come with living up here.

Excited to be sharing some of my feral habits in this new mini-series!

Ever since I learned to read and write, I’ve wanted to be an author. I remember sitting in the corner of a kindergarten, leafing through a children’s book and thinking: “one day, I’ll write one of these.” Can’t believe that dream is actually coming true now.

Pub day is getting closer and closer 😍 You can pre-order my first memoir, The Wilder Way, wherever you get your books, and it will be with you on 7 May if you’re in the UK 🇬🇧 and on 2 June if you’re in the USA 🇺🇸 . Links to online stores on my Instagram profile!

🇳🇱 The Dutch translation is also coming on 7th May
🇵🇱 Polish and 🇩🇪 German translations coming in October.

I’ll be sharing news about the book tour very soon - though if you’re subscribed to my newsletter, then you already know about the dates and locations 😍 Can’t wait to meet you all!!!

items.caption_text:
My rewilding journey started with my feet: going barefoot became my gateway into living in harmony with my body and my environment. It just made sense to rewild myself from the ground up.

Soon after my dive into barefoot living, I stopped wearing make-up, re-evaluated my relationship with body hair, started trail running and dreamt up the wild life I’m living today.

In between truly barefoot moments, I’ve been a faithful fan of @vivobarefoot shoes for 4 years now. I’ve hiked up mountains, ran ultra marathons and travelled across the continents in my Vivos.

It may seem like a small change but for me, barefoot shoes help me feel more wild and free on a daily basis.

So excited to finally join the @vivobarefoot team as their official ambassador ❤️

#freeyourfeet #vivobarefoot #paidpartnership

Once you’ve spent sufficient time overlanding, here are some things you’ll never take for granted again:
🚿 hot showers
🧺 fresh laundry 
🔋 being plugged into the grid 
🔧 not having breakdown anxiety 
🚽 toilets 

All of the above, I now consider some of life’s greatest luxuries. (No, really).

items.caption_text:
Locust swarms are not pleasant to drive through, but aside from inconveniencing drivers, they’re actually a massive problem for the local agriculture across Mauritania, Western Sahara and Morocco.

Along these desert stretches, they are known to obliterate already scarce crops. A single swarm - numbering in the tens of millions - can eat as much as 35,000 people in A SINGLE DAY. 

There’s something very end-of-time-biblical about seeing clouds of locust rising above the roads.

And yes, I had to remove every single insect from various nooks and crannies inside Odyssey by hand. 1/10 would not recommend.

Along these desert stretches, they are known to obliterate already scarce crops. A single swarm - numbering in the tens of millions - can eat as much as 35,000 people in A SINGLE DAY. 

There’s something very end-of-time-biblical about seeing clouds of locust rising above the roads.

And yes, I had to remove every single insect from various nooks and crannies inside Odyssey by hand. 1/10 would not recommend.

items.caption_text:
One of the absolute best things about this expedition has been sharing it with my best friend. Every day, Vilk made sure to cheer me up, made me feel safe and remind me to enjoy every little moment.

Travelling in the desert with a dog isn’t straightforward though. You need to bring extra water & backup food supplies, travel in specific seasons (when it’s cooler), be mindful of water safety, watch out for desert animals, even check the temperature of the sand (it can get hot in the afternoons!). 

And then there’s all the documents and paperwork involved in international travel with an animal, navigating different attitudes towards dogs in different cultures, finding quality vet care when necessary…

It takes extra prep, but it’s always worth it. My favourite trips are those I can take with Vilk.

We’ve been travelling together for nearly 4 years. From the north of Alaska to Mexico, from the Arctic Circle in winter to the Sahara Desert… 3 continents and countless adventures later, @vilksadventures will forever be my favourite adventure companion.

Thanks buddy for being by my side, I love you more than words can express ❤️

items.caption_text:
This specific oasis was my driving force for so much of Expedition Sahara. I had no idea what it actually looked like - I didn’t even know if it still existed - but every time I felt tired or frustrated on the road, I imagined myself right here, under the shade of the palm trees.

I was always headed towards this oasis, from the moment my wheels left home.

When I finally made it after 6 weeks of driving through the sands, the reality exceeded all my expectations. I’ve been travelling to offbeat places for 9 years full-time, but this must be one of the most stunning places I’ve ever seen with my own eyes.

Sometimes, it pays to follow your intuition towards the unknown.

Yes. Yes to all these things. 

📸 Cultural note: In these self-portraits, I’m wearing a melhfa - the traditional robe worn by women from various tribes and communities across the Sahara. I bought mine from a couple of ladies here in the desert in Mauritania, who dressed me up as we giggled and chatted in a mix of French and Arabic.

It never fails to amaze me how easy it is to find common ground with women across the world, despite the fact that our realities may look completely different. Grateful for that universal bond of girlhood and womanhood ❤️

items.caption_text:
Going into my 30s as a woman has come with a fair share of “well-meaning” warnings from people:

“You’ll end up alone if you keep living like that”

“Better settle now, cause nobody will want you once you’re *past your prime*”

“You’ll regret your choices”

But I have no anxiety at all about the life choices I’ve made. They’ve rendered my life free, open, full.

Not everyone will see it the same way. For many people, the way to a full life will look very different from yours.

And if you dare to stray too far from convention, you’ll probably be met with incredulity, suspicion, or disappointment. Sometimes, all three at the same time.

These reactions can hurt.

But you know what hurts more? Waking up 10 years later and realizing you were so afraid of disappointing the people around you that you ended up unhappy, burnt out and regretful.

At least, that was my thinking when I threw away my entire life years ago, disappointing and baffling my family and loved ones along the way. 

Nine years on, I know I’d made the right choice back then.

(Full story in my book The Wilder Way, coming to bookstores in May)

Reel idea inspo: @our.life.in.miles

items.caption_text:
Power List Spotlight: @evazubeck

The 34-year-old adventure YouTuber and National Geographic TV show host Eva zu Beck joins our Zoom call from a typically intrepid setting: the middle of the Sahara in Morocco, where she’s sitting out a sandstorm inside a converted Land Rover Defender. Soon, zu Beck and her German Shepherd, Wilk (whose name means “wolf” in Polish), will set off on a solo road trip across northwestern Africa, spending most of their time exploring the backroads of Morocco and Mauritania.

While a typical day for zu Beck nowadays might look like hauling rucksacks up Mount Kilimanjaro with a team of female porters or running a 319-mile ultramarathon through Arctic Lapland—both of which she has done in the past year—it wasn’t too long ago that she was clocking in at her high-pressure, corporate 9-to-5. In order to become a “feral woman”—the term she uses to describe her current state, her most authentic self—the Oxford grad had to leave behind her media start-up job and marriage in London with very little prior outdoors experience. “Back then, I had never slept alone in a tent,” she says. “My first solo camping experience was to take two horses out into the Mongolian wilderness on my own for two weeks.”

It’s a trajectory she documents in her upcoming memoir, 'The Wilder Way,' which she hopes will give other women the confidence and tool set to explore both less-trodden lifestyles and travel destinations. “A lot of these books and Hollywood movies about women, after they've gone out into the world and experienced this freedom and ‘found themselves,’ so to speak, they come back, find their Prince Charming, settle down, and life is beautiful again,” she says. “That's not my narrative.”

Read the full 2026 Women Who Travel Power List at the link in bio.

✍️: @hannah_taking_notes
📸: @evazubeck

items.caption_text:
No, it’s not “just a joke”. It’s a reflection of an attitude that’s still extremely common.

The mocking comments, the group-think, the enthusiastic compliance, the roaring laughter at the mere idea that women could be recognized for their achievements. It’s not just a joke.

Mockery - exactly like this - is part of the reason why women have been slower to enter the world of adventure and exploration. Because the prevailing attitude for far too long has been that women are too weak, too delicate, too feeble for such endeavours. 

BS.

The world of sports and adventure has been opening up and changing for the better - but clearly, there is so much more work to do.

May this small selection of incredibly powerful women guide you and inspire you 🤍

items.caption_text:
Slow mornings. Intentional movement. A little lift. Upneeq for low-lying eyelids fits seamlessly into my morning routine. Upneeq.com #Upneeq #ad #sponsored

Upneeq® (oxymetazoline hydrochloride ophthalmic solution), 0.1% is a prescription eyedrop used to treat acquired blepharoptosis (low-lying eyelids) in adults. ​

If you have drooping eyelid(s), talk to your doctor about your medical history, symptoms, and other medications you are taking. Drooping lids can be caused by other serious conditions of the eye, brain, and nerves, such as stroke. If you have heart disease, blood pressure or blood flow issues, or risk for angle-closure glaucoma, use care when taking Upneeq and call your doctor if your condition gets worse. ​

Each Upneeq vial is for one-time use. Use as directed and discard after use. Do not touch the tip of the vial to your eye or other surface.​
 
For additional Important Safety Information and Full Prescribing Information, visit link in @upneeq bio. ​

Paid partnership with RVL Pharmaceuticals.

items.caption_text:
Baba, we made history. 🥹 For years, I’ve shared little pockets of peace from my home… never imagining they would one day become something bigger.

Today, I’m so honored to share that I will be hosting my very own lifestyle series, Hosted by Nabela Noor, premiering May 20 on @Tubi 🤍

To be the first South Asian to host a lifestyle show for a major network is something I don’t take lightly.

This show is an invitation into my world - from the kitchen, to the garden, to the table…
and all the beauty in between.

It’s about romanticizing the everyday,
gathering with intention and creating a life that feels as beautiful as it looks.

And for little me, who never saw herself reflected in spaces like this… as the daughter of Bangladeshi immigrants who came to this country with everything to hope for and everything to build… this moment means the world.

To do this alongside my sister, @NeharikaNoor who is directing the show & producing it in-house as a family is the gift of a lifetime, and I know my Baba wouldn’t have wanted it any other way. 

This wouldn’t have been possible without God, the support of my family and every single one of you. 

I hope when you watch, you feel inspired to celebrate your life in the smallest, most meaningful ways and to treat every day like a special occasion. 

I can’t wait to host you.👒

raw.caption:
Baba, we made history. 🥹 For years, I’ve shared little pockets of peace from my home… never imagining they would one day become something bigger.

Today, I’m so honored to share that I will be hosting my very own lifestyle series, Hosted by Nabela Noor, premiering May 20 on @Tubi 🤍

To be the first South Asian to host a lifestyle show for a major network is something I don’t take lightly.

This show is an invitation into my world - from the kitchen, to the garden, to the table…
and all the beauty in between.

It’s about romanticizing the everyday,
gathering with intention and creating a life that feels as beautiful as it looks.

And for little me, who never saw herself reflected in spaces like this… as the daughter of Bangladeshi immigrants who came to this country with everything to hope for and everything to build… this moment means the world.

To do this alongside my sister, @NeharikaNoor who is directing the show & producing it in-house as a family is the gift of a lifetime, and I know my Baba wouldn’t have wanted it any other way. 

This wouldn’t have been possible without God, the support of my family and every single one of you. 

I hope when you watch, you feel inspired to celebrate your life in the smallest, most meaningful ways and to treat every day like a special occasion. 

I can’t wait to host you.👒

Transforming a forgotten corner of my third floor into a dreamy spa sanctuary. 🕊️

Just across from my Pilates studio, I designed a quiet retreat for at-home massages. What was once an empty space now feels like a small resort moment, layered with pieces that tell a story. 

Sometimes the most transformative design isn’t about adding square footage… it’s about seeing the spaces we already have with a little more awe and wonder.

Next for the third floor…the gym of my dreams. Would you like to see? 

#design #interiors #transformation #homedecor #diy

items.caption_text:
Love doesn’t shine in perfect moments — it grows in the ones that test you.

Когда мы готовились к годовщине свадьбы в Иордании, решили сделать особенные поздравления друг для друга и написать письма. 

Я всё больше убеждаюсь: любовь видна не в лёгких днях, а в тех, где нужно пройти сложные моменты. И где вместо громких слов приходят настоящие поступки. Не в кадрах с Мальдив, где у вас и так всё хорошо, не в ярких эмоциональных вспышках, а в моменте, когда вы всегда выбираете идти рядом, даже если трудно. Когда доверяеетсь этому тёплому живому свету, который ведёт вас через всё. 

И, может быть, именно там — в простых, несовершенных, но честных днях — любовь становится настоящей? ❤️

Song: @dinaram108

items.caption_text:
On the second day of the Kora, Dolma La Pass tested me with hail, rain, and thunder. I was second to last to cross.

On the third day, the path softened. My body found its rhythm, and my mind… space.

Kailash doesn’t care about your speed or strength.
It asks for presence.
For honesty.
For letting go.

Some journeys take you somewhere.
This one brings you back — to yourself

————————

Во второй день Коры перевал Долма Ла испытал меня градом, дождём и громом. Я прошёл его предпоследним.

Кайласу всё равно на твою скорость или силу.
Он требует присутствия.
Честности.
Умения отпускать.

Есть путешествия, которые ведут тебя куда-то.
А это — возвращает к самому себе

#TravelAwakened #FollowmetoTourTibet #tibet

items.caption_text:
➤ You don’t choose Kailash. Kailash chooses you 

6 years ago, Tibet changed something deep inside me. 
This summer, the call came again - louder, clearer. 

➤ Two weeks across sacred Tibetan lands. Three days walking the Kora - the 60km pilgrimage around Mount Kailash, believed to cleanse karma, strip ego, and open the heart to the truth within.

This wasn’t just a journey. It was a return to silence, to questions that matter, to the mountain that remembers. 
#TravelAwakened #FollowmetoTourTibet

items.caption_text:
What if the night sky became your ceiling, and the desert your bed?
On Socotra, we didn’t just visit—we surrendered.

We slept on sand dunes under a million stars.
We dined in the heart of ancient Dragon Blood Tree forests, where silence holds memory.
We played music and meditated under galaxies that reminded us how small—and how infinite—we are.
There were no walls, no noise, no time.
Just wind, shadows, and the soft rhythm of presence.

@followmeto TOUR to SOCOTRA

items.caption_text:
✸ Socotra will always be in our hearts ✸

♦ Сокотра не просто остров. Это зеркало ♦
Здесь невозможно спрятаться за ролью, за планами, за комфортом. Ты не спрячешь ни эмоции, ни усталость, ни правду о себе. Когда сбрасываются маски, остаётся только суть.

Кого-то вскрывает болью, кого-то — осознанием.
Но каждый получает свою трансформацию и опыт - не тот, за которым, как тебе казалось ты приехал - а тот, к которому ты был готов. 

Для меня это была проработка границ. Переоценка целей.
Напоминание, что вести за собой — значит не подстраиваться, а держать опору внутри.

♦Сокотра — не для всех. Но если она впускает — ты уже не уедешь прежним ♦

I was doing everything “right”. Balancing blood sugar, managing stress, prioritizing sleep, and I still felt flat. Foggy. Off.

That’s when I stopped guessing and started looking at my actual data.

Through @function, I tested 100+ biomarkers and found things a standard physical would have completely missed: off-balance thyroid markers, low vitamin D, elevated cortisol, and low B12. Each one a piece of the puzzle that explained exactly how I was feeling.

That’s what personalized health actually means to me. Not following the most “perfect” plan, but knowing your numbers and making changes that are specific to you.

Function gives you 160+ tests a year, results in plain language, no insurance, no referrals. It’s a dollar a day to actually know what’s going on inside your body. 

Use code BEWELL25 at functionhealth.com/bewellbykelly to get started.

items.caption_text:
Your body has a built-in off-switch for salt.⁠
⁠
⚡ When sodium is low, cravings spike.⁠
⚡ When you're replete, salt loses its appeal, and neural and hormonal signals shut the appetite down... until ultra-processed foods override it.⁠
⁠
We evolved powerful drives to seek salt and weak brakes on consuming it — because ancestrally, sodium was scarce and overdoing it wasn't a risk. Ultra-processed foods exploit that gap. They deliver sodium bundled with refined carbs, added sugars, and industrial fats — a formulation engineered to override satiety and keep you eating.⁠
⁠
This is why "just eat less salt" misses the point. Metabolically healthy people eating whole foods don't overconsume sodium — the off-switch works fine. ⁠
⁠
Case and point: Remove the salt from a bag of chips and it doesn't become a health food. The problem isn't sodium. It's everything else in the bag.

items.caption_text:
They’re not just in your water bottle. Microplastics are now being found in the brain, blood, placenta, and beyond. 🎙️

In this episode, Dr. Leonardo Trasande breaks down what the research is starting to show: tiny plastic particles are now being found throughout the body and scientists are still uncovering what that means for our long-term health.

We also get into how everyday exposures, like non-stick chemicals and PFAS, can disrupt thyroid function and metabolism, and why this might be one missing piece for people who feel like they’re doing everything “right” but still struggling with energy, hormones, or weight.

In this episode we unpack: 
→ Whether microplastics are actually harmful to humans 
→ How plastics and PFAS disrupt your hormones and endocrine system 
→ The connection between plastic exposure and metabolism and weight 
→ Where PFAS hide in your daily life and how to reduce exposure 
→ Simple, practical swaps you can make starting today

The goal is never fear. It’s knowledge. The more you understand what’s in your environment and how it interacts with your body, the better equipped you are to protect yourself and your family. 🤍

🎧 Link in stories to listen to the full episode now!

items.caption_text:
According to @bewellbykelly, there are some situations where a child's electrolyte balance may benefit from some extra support:⁠
💧 When they’re telling you they’re fatigued during activity⁠
💧 When they’re feeling nauseous⁠
💧 When they experience headaches⁠
💧 When they generally don’t feel great ⁠
⁠
A few guidelines for offering an electrolyte mix? Start with a lower dose. Dilute more than you would for yourself (because little bodies). Avoid mixes with added sugars.⁠
⁠
Water, food, and attention to the signals your child is giving you covers most situations. The rest? That's your call.

items.caption_text:
Quick story. I was reading every diet book on the shelf all through college. Then as a senior at USC, I learned about blood sugar balance and something clicked. When I started my career in cancer genetics and personalized medicine, I went even deeper. Mining PubMed, studying biomarkers, connecting the dots.

In 2012, I took everything I’d learned. The science, the research, my education at USC. I paired it together into something simple enough to actually live by.

The Fab Four. And it freed me.

No more counting calories. No more chasing macros. No more grams, points, or perfect.

Four categories. Protein, fat, fiber, greens. That’s the whole framework. You build your plate from the foundation up with blood sugar in mind, and you stop chasing a number.

It’s how I feed myself. It’s how I feed my boys. It’s what I’ve taught clients for 14 years and two books in. It’s how I blend a smoothie. 

The questions are simple:
Where’s my protein?
Where’s my fat?
Can I add a little more fiber and greens?

That’s it. That’s the conversation.

And it travels. Vacation, restaurants, birthday parties, takeout on a Tuesday. The Fab Four comes with you. No app, no tracking, no white-knuckling through a menu.

Swipe through for the framework. And if this is your first time hearing about it, start with one meal tomorrow. Just one. Notice what shifts.

Which meal are you Fab Four-ing first? 👇
#fabfour #bewellbykelly #bloodsugarbalance #fab4smoothie​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

items.caption_text:
The creatine craze is real these days, and if you’re wondering if it’s actually worth the hype… this post is for you.

I did a full Q&A with @bewellbykelly on the blog breaking down everything you guys asked about — what it is, who it’s for, the myths (bloating, weight gain, etc.), and how to actually take it. These slides are just a quick summary, but the blog post goes way deeper. A very worthwhile read!

I’ve been taking it consistently and yes, I’ve noticed a difference in my strength and energy in workouts — but what really surprised me is how much it’s helped with my overall energy, mental clarity, and less brain fog. A big win for us tired moms! 

✨Comment CREATINE and I’ll send you the full post + a link to the Be Well one I take every day.

My go-to transition drink just got a new flavor and I am so here for it. 🍓

You know those moments between school drop-off and your next meeting, or running from one appointment to the next, that’s when I reach for this. It’s not a meal replacement, it’s just real, clean fuel to get me from point A to point B.

Protein is the first thing I build every meal and snack around because it’s what keeps your blood sugar stable, your energy steady, and your hunger hormones in check. Without it, you’re running on empty and reaching for whatever’s in front of you. This is how I stay ahead of that.

30 grams of complete protein, A2 dairy, lactose-free, zero seed oils, and no ingredients I can’t pronounce. 

@pioneer.pastures Strawberry Milk Protein Shake just launched at Target and Amazon and it’s the only ready-to-drink protein I trust enough to drink myself and recommend to you. 🤍

items.caption_text:
Why do we overreact in relationships, shut down emotionally, or constantly put everyone else first, even when we know it’s not what we actually want for ourselves? 💭

In this episode of Be Well by Kelly podcast, I sit down with Dr. Nicole LePera, holistic psychologist and bestselling author, to discuss her new book Reparenting the Inner Child: The New Science of Our Oldest Wounds and How to Heal Them, where she explores how early emotional experiences shape the patterns we carry into adulthood, and how we can begin to heal them in a grounded, practical way. 🌿

✨ We break down:
+ how the “inner child” shows up in your relationships
why you may repeat emotional cycles without realizing it
+ how even well-intentioned parenting can shape these patterns
+ what actually helps shift them: nervous system awareness + regulation tools
+ small, intentional changes that support you in not abandoning yourself 🧠✨

This conversation is a powerful reminder that you are not broken—you are patterned, and patterns can be changed. 💛

If you’ve ever felt stuck in the same relational cycles, this episode will completely shift how you understand yourself, and what healing actually looks like. Link in stories to listen 🎧

items.caption_text:
Wait… cookies that support your blood sugar? 🍪 
I know — I was skeptical too.

But this is basically a Fab Four smoothie… in cookie form.
Protein, fat, fiber… and yes, chocolate. 😉

And here’s the little upgrade:

You can skip the banana.

The grass-fed beef protein isolate has naturally occurring collagen, so you still get that soft, chewy texture without needing the extra sugar.

Which means these are higher protein, lower sugar… and actually keep you full.

All you do:
1 cup Peanut butter, 3 scoops of vanilla be well by Kelly protein powder and chocolate chips.

The original recipe calls for a banana, and I tested it but didn’t love it. I would stick to 1 small of 1/2 of a medium max too much an they a gooey. If your peanut butter is too oily a little flax meal will add fiber and be the perfect fix. 

Bake at 350 for about 12 minutes…
And you’ve got a cookie that tastes good and feels good.
Save this one — you’re going to want it 🤍 

Earth Day 🌎 Sale = Love2earth for 20% off

items.caption_text:
Chili Crispy Rice Salad 🍚🌶️🥬

This is my new favorite Fab 4 lunch! Golden chili-crisp rice + fresh crunchy veggies, herbs, ground turkey, and creamy peanut sauce. It’s crispy, crunchy, creamy, herby, and keeps you full & balanced for hours.

Ingredients (2–3 generous servings)
Crispy Chili Rice
•  2 cups cooked & cooled jasmine rice (day-old best)
•  1 heaping tbsp Momofuku chili crunch
•  Generous pinch of salt (~¼–½ tsp)
Ground Turkey
•  1 lb ground turkey
•  2 garlic cloves, minced
•  1 tbsp minced fresh ginger
•  Coconut aminos (a few good splashes, to taste)
•  Salt to taste
Salad
•  ½ head green cabbage, shredded
•  2 Persian cucumbers, sliced
•  ½ cup shelled edamame
•  1 avocado, diced
•  ¼ cup toasted coconut flakes
•  ¼ cup roasted salted peanuts, chopped
•  2 tbsp each: chopped fresh Thai basil, mint & cilantro
Peanut Sauce (from my Thai salad in Body Love Every Day)
•  3 tbsp natural peanut butter
•  2 tbsp coconut aminos (or tamari)
•  1–2 tbsp fresh lime juice
•  1 tbsp honey or maple syrup
•  1 tsp chili crunch or sriracha (optional)
•  1–3 tbsp water (to thin to creamy drizzly consistency)
Instructions
1.  Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss rice with chili crunch + salt. Spread on a baking sheet and bake 25–35 min until golden & crispy, stirring halfway.
2.  In a skillet, mash and cook turkey with garlic, ginger, coconut aminos, and salt until browned.
3.  In a large bowl, add cabbage, cucumbers, edamame, avocado, coconut, peanuts, and herbs.
4.  Whisk peanut sauce until smooth, adding water as needed.
5.  Add turkey + crispy rice to the bowl, drizzle with sauce, toss gently, and serve immediately while the rice is warm & crunchy!
Every bite hits the Fab 4 — protein, fat, fiber & greens. Save this & tag me if you make it! Who’s trying it? Drop a 🔥 below or comment mash to snag my masher!👇

items.caption_text:
If you feel exhausted after eating, your metabolism is trying to tell you something 👇

Energy crashes. Constant cravings. Weight that won’t move. Sound familiar?

This week on the podcast, I sat down with Andrew Koutnik, PhD, metabolic researcher and expert, to break down what’s actually happening beneath the surface… and what you can do about it. 🎙️

We talk about:
+ Why feeling tired after meals can be a sign of metabolic inflexibility
+ The truth about insulin resistance + blood sugar regulation
+ Why high blood pressure isn’t just about sodium (it’s the combo of glucose + insulin, too)
+ How liquid calories and sugary drinks spike blood sugar without giving anything back
+ And how to use nutrition as a tool to support energy, weight, and long-term health

Andrew also shares insights from decades of research, and his own experience living with type 1 diabetes, on how powerful nutrition can be when you understand what your body actually needs. 

This is one of those conversations that shifts how you look at your plate… and your glass. 🍹

New episode is live. Link in stories to listen! 🤍

items.caption_text:
Magnesium is one of those nutrients we don’t talk about enough… but it plays a major role in brain health 🧠✨

Recent research shows that low magnesium levels are associated with a higher risk of cognitive decline, including dementia and Alzheimer’s.

Why? Because magnesium is essential for things like synaptic communication (how your brain cells talk to each other), regulating inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress—all key factors in how our brain ages.

When magnesium levels are low, it can contribute to increased inflammation, impaired memory pathways, and even the buildup of harmful proteins in the brain. Over time, that creates an environment that may accelerate cognitive decline.

The takeaway?

This isn’t about fear, it’s about awareness. Supporting your body with the nutrients it needs now is one of the most powerful ways to protect long-term brain health.
While more research is always needed, maintaining healthy magnesium levels may be a simple, supportive tool for cognitive function as we age.

Comment MAGNESIUM and I’ll send you my go-to 🤍

items.caption_text:
I know that sounds too simple. But hear me out.

When you eat a high-carb breakfast or you skip it and over eat at lunch your blood sugar takes the hit….your glucose spikes fast and crashes hard. By 10am your body is screaming for carbs or if held out it’s backfiring and you’re looking for chocolate and caffeine at 2pm. That’s not a discipline problem. That’s biology.

The Fab Four is how I fix it. Every meal, I build around four things:

⭐️Protein — to stabilize blood sugar and keep you full 
⭐️Fat — to slow digestion and satisfy your brain 
⭐️Fiber — to feed your gut and steady the glucose curve ⭐️Greens — to add micronutrients without spiking anything

That’s it.

No calorie counting. No food rules. No “good” and “bad” lists.

Just four categories that change how your body processes every single meal.

I feed my kids this way. I eat this way. My clients eat this way. Not because it’s trendy but because the 3pm crash disappears. The brain fog lifts and sugar cravings stop running the show.

You don’t have to be perfect. You just have to start with one meal.

Tomorrow morning: protein, fat, fiber, greens. See how you feel by lunch, see how it impacts those 3pm crashes. 

Then come back and tell me. I want to hear it!

I feed my kids this way. I eat this way. My clients eat this way. Not because it’s trendy but because the 3pm crash disappears. The brain fog lifts and sugar cravings stop running the show.

You don’t have to be perfect. You just have to start with one meal.

Tomorrow morning: protein, fat, fiber, greens. See how you feel by lunch, see how it impacts those 3pm crashes. 

Then come back and tell me. I want to hear it!

items.caption_text:
I’m always thinking about how to Fab Four everything… so I’m starting a series called Let’s Fab Four That 🙌

Whether it’s a homemade recipe or you’re reaching for a box of Simple Mills @simplemills or Go Nannas @go_nanas (my two faves), you can easily add protein, fat, fiber and greens (or Whole Foods…we’re looking for produce here!) to turn any meal or snack into something that actually keeps you and your kids full longer and snacking less. 

Swipe to see exactly how I do it. It’s all about blood sugar balance because when your blood sugar is stable, your energy is sustained and so is your sanity. 😄

⭐️ add protein and fiber to recipes when you can
⭐️ skip the sugar when you can or use natural sources like bananas, or the sweetness that comes through your protein powder, which is organic monk fruit and has no effect on your blood sugar
⭐️ the fewer ingredients the better, try to opt for grain free or less flour options
⭐️ ask yourself do these have protein, fat, fiber, and or added Whole Foods? 

Use the slider or save this for your next meal prep! 👇 what do you want to see me Fab Four next?

items.caption_text:
Mauna Lani on the Big Island — here’s what I’d absolutely do again 👇🏼

@maunalaniauberge — It’s incredibly kid-friendly in the best way. Think sandlot-style baseball games, footballs flying in the pool, nonstop giggles and splashing… all wrapped in a beautiful, elevated resort that truly caters to families. The kids make dozens of friends and run to lawn like it is recess. 

🌴 Canoe House — steak, fried rice, veggie sides… and the kids can run and play on the lawn right out front 🙌🏼 
🌴 HaLani Mediterranean — skewers, fresh salads, tagines (so good) 
🌴 Juice 101 — an easy walk from the hotel to build your own #fab4smoothie
🌴 Surf Shack — snorkel + stand-up paddle right out front, and during Oct–March you can surf there too. Perfect for a casual lunch after (burgers, tacos, and a killer Chinese chicken salad) it’s great for casual sunset dinner with live music. 

🛶 Sunrise paddle — we didn’t make it this trip (next time!), but Sebastian and I took the canoe out at 8am to snorkel the reef… and got up close with 2 sea turtles + hundreds of fish 
🎾 Grab a pickleball or tennis court for a little movement 
⛳️ 9-hole course for kids — we played more than once 
🚲 Rent bikes and cruise to the village (Juice 101!) or the tennis club

Francis Brown Club: this gives you access to their club room, which is basically an open bar (nice if your hubby is large and likes 🍻) coffee bar, pantry loaded with snacks think a refrigerator with hummus and veggies turkey sandwiches, a candy bar and then special events and homemade cookies/wine tastings. I think it totally makes sense if you’re staying at the hotel and you don’t plan on leaving, but Id double check their schedule to make sure it aligns with your lifestyle. 

Already dreaming about going back 💛

*We had so many plans to head off property, but everything is so spread out here — it’s about a 30-minute drive on average to get anywhere — so we ultimately decided laying low with the kids was the way to go. We did make it to Tommy Bahama for dinner, which is only about 10 minutes away, and it was a great easy night out. Just a heads up: it gets hot, so ask for a shaded table or go at sunset; 5pm was toasty.

items.caption_text:
You’re eating well. You’re staying consistent. You’re doing all the “right” things… and still finding yourself hungry an hour after lunch, reaching for something sweet at 3pm, and wondering why the scale isn’t moving.

If that sounds familiar, hear this: you are not broken, and this is not a willpower problem. 💛

Comment FUNG and I’ll send you the full episode 🎧

In this episode, I sit down with Jason Fung, physician, bestselling author, and one of the leading voices in metabolic health, for a conversation that completely reframes how we think about hunger, cravings, and weight.

We break down how food actually impacts the body, through hormones and the nervous system, not just calories on a label. 🧠⚡️

Because 500 calories of cookies is not the same as 500 calories of broccoli. Once you eat, your body triggers completely different hormonal responses, especially insulin, which plays a major role in fat storage and energy balance.

This is exactly why I always come back to the Fab Four, protein, fat, fiber, and greens, to support blood sugar balance, steady energy, and lasting satiety. 🥑🥬

In this episode, we cover:
 ✨ Why calories in vs. calories out doesn’t tell the full story
 🧬 How insulin acts as your primary fat-storage hormone
 🍽 The difference between physical, hormonal, and conditioned hunger
 🥯 How ultra-processed foods are designed to trigger all three
 📉 Why blood sugar instability drives cravings
 🧠 Why food addiction is a physiological response, not a character flaw

This is one of those conversations that shifts everything, because your body isn’t working against you, it’s responding exactly how it’s designed to. 💫

Comment FUNG and I’ll send you the full episode 🎧

If you’re lying in bed exhausted, but your brain won’t turn off…this is for you 😴✨

⤵️ Drop MAGNESIUM to shop my nightly non-negotiable for brain and body calm. 

A double-blind, placebo-controlled study (the gold standard in research) looked at what happens when people struggling with insomnia take magnesium for 8 weeks…

And the results? 👀
💤 They fell asleep faster
💤 They slept more efficiently (less tossing + turning)
💤 They stayed asleep longer
💤 They woke up feeling more refreshed

But here’s where it gets really powerful. Magnesium didn’t just “help them relax,” it actually changed key sleep hormonal function:
🌙 Melatonin increased (the sleep hormone)
📉 Cortisol decreased (thestress hormone)
More sleep hormone…less stress hormone.

If you’ve been struggling with:
• waking up between 2-4am
• feeling tired but unable to fall asleep
• light, restless sleep
• or that “I slept but I’m still exhausted” feeling
👉 this is your sign to look at magnesium.

Here’s the reality most don’t know: We burn through magnesium daily (stress, caffeine, workouts, hormone function can all deplete magnesium stores!), and most of us are already running low.

This means the body literally doesn’t have the raw materials it needs to calm your nervous system and produce sleep hormones properly.

Be Well Magnesium offers 500 mg magnesium blend along with synergistic co-factors GABA and L-theanine for calm and relaxation. 

Cutting edge sucrosomial magnesium means:
→ Higher bioavailability

→ Effective cellular delivery = better clinical outcomes

Make good sleep your norm. Give your body the right support 💛

items.caption_text:
There’s a version of me I meet on vacation… and I really like her.

She’s slower. Softer. Not rushing everyone out the door or thinking three steps ahead. She says “yes” more. She lingers. She laughs easier. She plays.

And if I’m being honest, I feel a little guilty when I notice the difference.

Like… why can’t I be this mom all the time?

But I also know this is real life. There are seasons of building, pushing, creating—and then moments where we get to exhale and remember what it’s all for.

This week, I’ve been soaking it up. Watching my kids, being present with Chris, feeling like the version of me they deserve more of.

And instead of just wishing I could hold onto this feeling, I’m actually building a plan to bring her home with me.
Getting smarter with my time. Using AI to take things off my plate, building agents, scheduling tasks….all of it. Simplifying meals, simplifying goals, focusing on the meaning of my life. 

And creating systems that give me space back.

Because “vacation me” isn’t a different person.
She just shuts down work, and she’s less overwhelmed.

And I think we all deserve to feel a little more like that… even in the middle of real life.

items.caption_text:
Is there an Easter treat more iconic than Peeps?! 🐣

As a lifelong lover of nostalgic treats, I’m on a mission to recreate the classics with a healthier twist. I want my boys to experience the magic of holiday foods like Peeps, only with ingredients I can actually get behind.

These homemade marshmallow Peeps are everything. Fluffy, fun, and sweet without the spike, no artificial dyes, no preservatives, no refined sugar, no corn syrup. Just real ingredients made with a lot more love than anything you’ll find in a cardboard box.

I sweetened these with either honey or allulose, your call. Honey gives a gentle bump in blood sugar, while allulose has nearly zero effect. Both versions are delicious, both versions are clean, and both versions will make your kids (and your inner child) very happy.

Trust me, these are as fun to make as they are to eat. 

Comment PEEPS below for the recipe! 🐣🐣🐣

items.caption_text:
I’ve been seeing homemade peanut butter eggs everywhere lately, and while I love the nostalgia, I always want a version that actually supports how I want to feel—stable energy, no crash, and a little protein to anchor it all.

These are simple, freezer-friendly, and made with just a few ingredients you probably already have on hand. The only added sugar comes from a little melted Hu chocolate on top, and the combo of healthy fats + protein (8g per egg) makes them way more satisfying than the traditional version.

Keep a batch in your freezer and you’ve got a feel-good treat ready all week (or something fun to make with your kids that won’t send them into a sugar spiral).

Because balance > deprivation… always.

Protein peanut butter eggs… because Easter 🐣 candy doesn’t have to be a blood sugar rollercoaster.

⅔ cup creamy peanut butter
2 scoops vanilla @bewellbykelly protein
1-4 tbsp almond flour
1 cup dark chocolate (I used chocolate gems, chips work too)
Maldon or kosher sea salt

Mix the peanut butter and protein together, then add almond flour a tablespoon at a time until it’s not sticky. Shape into eggs. Melt your chocolate and either drizzle on top or fully coat each egg. Sprinkle with sea salt. Freeze.

items.caption_text:
✨🥄 One tool, endless uses…

The Masher isn’t just for ground meat. It’s my secret weapon in the kitchen. Sleek, stainless steel, and dishwasher safe, it makes prep easier, cleanup faster, and cooking more fun. 

Here’s how I use it to elevate everyday cooking:

🍔 Crumble Ground Beef – perfectly even browning for tacos, soups, or bowls
 🍗 Shred Slow-Cooked Chicken – straight in the pot, no extra tools needed
 🍓 Smash Berries – homemade jam, add texture to smoothies, or top on yogurt bowls
 🥑 Mash Avocados – guacamole, toast, or baby-friendly mashed avocado
 🍋 Fresh Squeezed Juices – oranges, grapefruits, lemons, and more
 🥕 Soften Cooked Vegetables – potatoes, carrots, cauliflower, or squash
 🥔 Smashed Potatoes – par-cook, smash halfway, drizzle olive oil, bake for crispy edges
 🫘 Make Refried Beans – lightly mash for creamy beans without turning to paste
 🥚 Egg or Tuna Salad – gently mash for creamy, perfect tuna or egg salad sandwiches
 🧄 Smash Garlic – crush cloves fast for cooking or marinades
 🌿 Muddle Herbs, Cucumbers, or Jalapeños – flavor cocktails, mocktails, or dishes
 🍌 Mash Bananas & Mix Baked Goods – perfect for banana bread, muffins, and more

💬 How are you using The Masher in your kitchen??

👇Comment “MASHER” below to shop The Masher today (stock is limited!) and get my full top 12 ways to use The Masher — trust me, it’ll change the way you cook.

items.caption_text:
Creatine is everywhere right now, but quality is *not* the same, even if you’re sourcing from a well known US manufacturer. 

I only use Creavitalis — a German‑made, research‑grade, micronized creatine from Alzchem — for my family and my community. It’s produced in dedicated facilities with pharma‑level quality controls, the same manufacturer behind Creapure, it’s just the micronized version for better bioavailability, solubility and GI side effects. Everyone is quoting studies but this is the source used in most of the women’s creatine research we all referencing. 

Here’s the problem: most creatine on the market is cheap bulk powder, often from China, where independent testing has repeatedly found impurities and unwanted by‑products. Third‑party testing usually checks: “Is there creatine? Are heavy metals and microbials okay?” It often does *not* deeply screen for synthesis by‑products like DCD, DHT, or excess creatinine. So a product can be “third‑party tested” and still be made from low‑grade raw material.

Then there are gummies. Recent testing on popular creatine gummies found many with little to *no* actual creatine and significant breakdown into creatinine. So consumers think they’re getting an evidence‑based dose, but the form and sourcing completely undermine the science. 

And with doses going up, daily use becoming normal, and more teens and kids jumping on creatine, sourcing matters more than ever. If a brand isn’t clearly calling out Creavitalis or Creapure and exactly where their creatine is made, I assume they’re using anonymous commodity material and this included eve the best US manufacturers we all trust. 

You can fix your diet. You can fix your training. But you cannot “out‑train” contaminated or under‑dosed creatine. That’s why I call out my source and pay for this premium. I only trust German‑made Creavitalis for myself and my boys and I source every BWBK product this way, zero compromises period. 

https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9761713

People struggle making decisions without realizing that even if they think it through carefully, deeply, and smartly, many times they can’t control many of the other variables and they won’t know the outcome. 

This concept that you will ‘definitely know’ or are ‘sure of it’ is just a falsity. So, do the best you can; use your intuition, not just your brain. Make a call and move on. Speed matters. Adaptability matters.

#quotes #quoteoftheday #garyvee

Once you understand this truth as an owner … you and your employees can both win ❤️❤️❤️‼️ pass this onto a business owner … When I was a kid my dad used to talk about being upset about his employees not working as hard as him. Even as a 16-year-old I turned to my father and told him, “Well they will never work as hard as you if they don’t own the business.” This is a lost understanding among so many owners. We must not have the audacity or the selfishness to expect this. We must work for them once we work for them and understand what they want, whether it’s money or vacation time or status or a bigger title. Once we work for them they will work for us.

#garyvee #buisness #hiring
