This is for all the people in the arena ( your life, your career, your family ) who are doing it for them .. not for the validation of the crowd 

One of the great issues in our society right now is that people live their lives for outside validation, for the clicks, for the likes, for the followers, for the comments. This isn’t just in social media. This is happening in the analog and real world too. Too many people are keeping up with the Joneses and worried about what people think about them. They worry about what people think about how much money they have, what school they go to, or even worse, what school their kids go to, and on and on and on … I will continue to make content with the hope it breaks someone out of the hamster wheel and that’s that ❤️❤️❤️❤️

#success #mindset #garyvee

I hope this puts things in perspective… Whatever little things you’re worried about right now like the judgment of a stranger because they left a comment that upset you, or a fight you’re having with a friend or acquaintance that will blow over next week, or a mistake that you made at work, or a challenge you have from a client, or a big test or grade that’s coming up that in ten years will mean nothing, or a fight you’re having with a best friend that eventually will be a joke you make about in the future.

Or the millions and millions of mistakes we make every day as humans about worrying about dumb shit like someone cutting you off in line or bumping into you or all the other silly shit that we, as specks in the universe, continue to make when none of it really matters. I hope this post wakes you the fuck up

items.caption_text:
I remember when my wife told me she believed I was meant to become a billionaire and honestly, it irritated me.

From where I stood, I was already maxed out. I was grinding every day, sacrificing, pushing harder than anyone around me. I wasn’t coasting, I was all in. So when she said that, it didn’t feel like encouragement. It felt like more pressure. Like somehow, everything I was doing still wasn’t enough.

What I didn’t see at the time was her perspective.

She wasn’t questioning my work ethic. She wasn’t saying I needed to try harder. She was looking at my potential and realizing my targets were too small for the level of drive I had. And that’s a frustrating place to be, when your ambition is bigger than the goals you’ve given yourself. You end up working like crazy but still feeling like something’s missing.

Back then, I thought I was pushing my limits. In reality, I had just gotten comfortable with a version of them.

Elena wasn’t trying to make my life harder, she was trying to expand my vision. And that’s not always a comfortable experience. When someone sees more in you than you see in yourself, it forces you to confront the possibility that you’ve been playing smaller than you could.

That kind of push doesn’t always feel good in the moment. Sometimes it feels like friction. Sometimes it feels like pressure you don’t want. But looking back, I can see that it was necessary.

Because the truth is, when your capacity is high but your targets are low, that unused energy turns into frustration. You don’t feel fulfilled, you feel stuck, even though you’re working nonstop.

What I’ve learned is this, the strongest partnerships aren’t built on comfort. They’re built on growth. The right person in your corner won’t just support what you’re doing, they’ll challenge what you believe is possible.

And yeah, sometimes that’s annoying. Sometimes you resist it.

But if they really see you and they’re right, then that pressure you’re pushing against might be the exact thing you need to break through to the next level.

items.caption_text:
6 years ago I was stripped of my name, my money, my contacts and was given $100, a truck, a phone with no numbers and 90 days to prove one thing: that success is strategy, not luck.

Most people think you need connections, followers, or investors to win. I proved that with the right plan and relentless execution, you can turn $100 into a $5.5 million business in 90 days.

I didn’t do Undercover Billionaire to prove to other people I could do it, in all honesty I did it for myself... so I KNEW I could do it. Because once you know you can accomplish so much with so little, you know you are qualified to share that information with the world and really help other people do the same thing in their own lives.

This wasn’t about being comfortable. It was about being committed. Winners don’t wait for perfect conditions, they create them. They take every challenge, turn it into a lever, and use it to amplify action.

This weekend I am hosting a free webinar to share my 6 key takeaways from this experience and how you can use them to really expand and dominate the rest of 2026.

And no, you don’t need to shave your head, change your name, and be stripped down to your last $100 for this to work. Trust me.

Comment FREE to claim your free access to my webinar on Sunday.

items.caption_text:
Half my face aged in 3 weeks.
I’m a board-certified derm, and I quit every product on the left side of my face, except sunscreen, for 21 days.

The right side? My full routine.

The result: Visible photo damage reappears: sun spots + uneven tone. That is what aging looks like in real time, and that’s exactly the kind of damage a real skincare routine can prevent 👏

To everyone who says you can’t age this fast: skin doesn’t lie.

💬 Comment EVEN and I’ll DM you the exact routine I used on the right side.

Full methodology: same sleep, same diet, no driving (I’m in NYC, no car), SPF on both sides daily.

items.caption_text:
PSA: This is your sign to get your mammogram. I just got mine.

📊 1 in 8 women will get breast cancer. Caught early, ~99% survive.

🫣 And yet, so many still don’t go. Fear, avoidance, “later”… I get it. But early detection is everything.
👩‍⚕️ Start at 40 or earlier if high risk (talk to your doctor)

Ultrasound? Not a replacement, but can be added for dense breasts or specific concerns. (I do both.)

Go get your scan.

👇 Have you had yours? What was your experience? Any advice or questions?

items.caption_text:
Imagine being so triggered by… another language.

Thank you @annehathaway for spotlighting the word “Inshallah,” an Arabic word that transcends borders, cultures and religions and that signifies “god willing”, or culturally “hopefully” especially during Arab American Heritage Month 🥹

Growing up, I used to soften parts of my Arab identity because of how Arabs were portrayed - portrayal being the key word. Not because I wasn’t proud… but because it felt easier. Especially when a former employer once introduced me as being “from the French part of Washington DC” when patients asked about my accent 🤯

So if you’ve ever felt like you had to shrink parts of who you are to fit in: I see you ❤️ Maybe it’s time we unlearn that. Together. AMEEN 🙌

items.caption_text:
With the weight of everything happening right now, it feels strange to celebrate… but I’m allowing myself this small moment: two years at Sephora, and hope for what’s ahead 🙏

Pic 1: Today. Celebrating 2 years of @dr.idriss @sephora 
Pic 2: None of this could have ever been possible without all of you and as luck would have it - as I was standing for this picture a fellow nerd @vanearellanor came up to me and let me know how much our PillowtalkDerm family has helped her over the years 🥹 the significance of the timing was not lost me 
Pic 3: Forever grateful and honored to be a part of this journey alongside each one of you. I’ll be popping into Sephoras across NYC all throughout the sale (4/10-4/20). If you see me please say hi 💜
Pic 4: 2 years ago for our launch @sephora 
Pic 5: 1 year ago
Pic 6: 4 years ago raw footage of me balling my eyes out of fear, emotion and disbelief 2 min prior to launching our brand
Pic 7: … and back to work we go 😎

Never in my wildest dreams did I think my small, self-funded brand, built by an Arab woman with zero business background and zero investors, would have a place at all - let alone on shelves @sephora. Period. 

And this year’s picture and the moment captured behind it—might just be my favorite yet. Because it was never about me. It was always about YOU. Your trust. Your belief. Your support.

So, if you’re shopping the Sephora sale, and spot @dr.idriss in stores, please feel free to take a selfie and share with us because we’re celebrating with you, always.

From the bottom of my heart: THANK YOU!!!!!
Let’s keep building. Together. 🚀💜🔥

items.caption_text:
She’s tried Botox. She’s tried great under eye creams. Neither one treats under-eye hollowing. And nobody told her…

At 54, under-eye hollowing is almost always a structural volume problem — fat loss, bone density changes, and sometimes cheek descent depeening the whole area from below.

💉 Botox relaxes muscles
👀 Eye cream sits on the surface
🤷‍♀️ Neither one is reaching the issue

The point is: diagnois first, treatment second. Always. 

Under-eye questions? Let me know below ⬇️ 

✍️Credit to @olha.maikina.md for the facial aging illustration

items.caption_text:
There’s no single miracle treatment…not even a facelift.

A facelift can lift and tighten, but it doesn’t fix skin quality, texture, or uneven tone.

When you expect one procedure to do everything, you’re not just wasting money: you’re missing the bigger picture. 🤷‍♀️ Questions? Ask below 

🎥 blue_eyes_444 on TikTok

P.S. The stupid TikTok beauty filter was on when I recorded this video and I didn’t realize until AFTER filming. All my videos are filter-free so please excuse this mistake 😭

items.caption_text:
Sunlight isn’t the enemy, but unprotected, cumulative UV damage is!

Some of you are confusing the benefits of sunlight with a free pass to skip sun protection… and those are not the same thing.

Wear the damn sunscreen and please don’t subscribe to this stupidity 🤦‍♀️

OG video of sun protected humans (not the stupidity) from @drjenntran

items.caption_text:
🥇Retinol is the gold standard for improving collagen, fine lines, and wrinkles. But the conversation around how often to use it? Not so black and white. 

🤓Here’s the truth:
The data for retinol is based on consistent, long-term use. There are no miracle overnight results.

🚨But if you’re NEW to the retinol game, that does NOT mean you jump in face first because your skin may not be ready to handle it every single night from day one.

The issue? If you go too hard and too fast → you could trigger irritation and inflammation. And irritation doesn’t accelerate results… it sets you back.

💡Retinoids work best when your skin can actually tolerate them.

👩‍⚕️So what does that mean for you?
Starting a few nights a week isn’t “less effective,” and ideally you’re working your way up to using it more nights a week than not. It’s how you build up to effective use without compromising your skin.

⏳ When should you start?
Late 20s / early 30s is great—but honestly, it’s never too late.

🙈 Biggest mistake I see?
Going wrong and strong straight for the strongest option without building tolerance which blows your face up making you give up altogether.

So repeat after me: 
CONSISTENCY OVER INTENSITY. 
Every. Single. Time.

💬 Drop your questions below 💙
And SAVE this for when you’re ready to start (or restart) a retinol.

items.caption_text:
🔥 Hot tip for clearer skin: If you got a little lazy with SPF this winter (it’s okay!) but now is the time to get back on it

Spring is when so many people, including my patients, start noticing their dark spots looking darker 👀 Why? Because even if it’s still cold AF where you live, the UV Index is creeping 🆙

🧴 If the UV Index is 3 or higher, sunscreen is a must.

📲 You can check the UV Index directly in your weather app. Just scroll down. Or, to keep things simple: just wear the damn sunscreen every day 👏

And if dark spots are your main concern, this is the time to get consistent with a targeted routine like the Major Fade Protocol.

Let me know if you have questions below. I’m here to help 🫶

🎥: skin_tia on TikTok

items.caption_text:
@CyrusVeyssi first went viral *by accident.* (Iconic.)

The internet’s self-proclaimed “bougie gay uncle” skyrocketed to fame for their hilarious, self-shot videos, which often feature their father, @YourFavoriteBaba.

“I started to build that character online and it became this sort of intersection between myself and a performance,” Veyssi tells host Kim Peiffer (@PeifferK1) on this week’s episode of #ForbesTaksShop. “Finding the places where I could incorporate my organic voice into joy and into characters has been my focus.”

This week, Veyssi kicks off Season Two to discuss the routines and tools that fuel their success. Watch the full episode at the link in our bio.

items.caption_text:
Sometimes it’s not a big life lesson, it’s just a small moment that stays with you. 🌸

We were just sitting at a café when he said no to a straw. I didn’t think much of it, honestly. Then he just said, “plastic hai, environment ke liye accha nahi hai.”

I’ve always been someone who gets bothered seeing garbage everywhere, but I never really questioned my own choices. That day it just clicked, I might not be able to remove all the waste around me, but I can at least stop adding to it where I can.

Now sometimes I pause before buying or taking something I don’t really need. Not in a dramatic way, just, a little more aware. It’s weird how such a random moment can shift something in you. 

#sustainablelifestyle #plasticfreeliving #zerowastejourney

items.caption_text:
What if your kitchen waste could replace chemical cleaners? 🌱
This Earth Day, I conducted a sustainability talk + bioenzyme workshop at Adobe, and discussed how we look at “waste”.

We turned simple citrus peels into a natural multipurpose cleaning solution — something our homes and the planet both benefit from.

Small shifts like this = big impact over time.
Would you try making bioenzymes at home? 👇

Save the peels from first batch and use it for 2nd batch, it works as a catalyst & your 2nd batch will be ready in 1 month! 

& If you don’t have time to make one, comment Bioenzyme & I’ll send you the list of homegrown brands that sell Bioenzyme products across India. 

#EarthDayEveryDay #SustainableLiving #ZeroWaste #EcoFriendly #BioEnzyme

items.caption_text:
Our city’s progress sometimes chokes the very nature we love. I’m on Dehradun’s Canal Road, where the new footpath thats under construction looks great, but there’s a catch. 

While I love that they’ve left space around the trees, we need to do better. Cemented raised beds & Covering every inch with concrete and sealing tiles with cement means the rainwater has nowhere to go but flood the streets! 🌧️

🌳Here’s how we can make Dehradun truly green 👇🏻 
🌱Percolated Tiles: Use porous tiles or leave gaps filled with sand and soil to let the ground absorb water.
🌱Remove the Cement: Stop choking tree bases with cemented raised beds & leftover construction mix!
🌱Native Trees only: Let’s plant local species instead of just ornamental ones.

These concerns were shared with @namami91.ias ji - Municipal Commissioner of Dehradun, by the @cfgd_dehradun community @ira_chauhan @sonia.pandey.98 @razdeep_ & Himanshu arora ji in hope for sustainable future development that works with nature. 

#Dehradun #GreenDoon #SustainableLiving #UrbanPlanning

items.caption_text:
The idea that a city can grow and still make space to breathe.
That development doesn’t always have to take, it can also give back.

Walking through the Chakkarpur–Wazirabad Bundh, you can actually feel that shift. It’s quieter, slower… almost like the city pauses for a second.

And knowing that spaces like this are being created alongside homes like Godrej Miraya makes it even more meaningful.

Because maybe the future of living isn’t just about better homes…
it’s about a better environment around them.

And that’s something worth being part of.

@godrejproperties_north 

#NeighboursWithNature #GodrejMiraya #SustainableLuxuryLiving #GodrejProperties #GodrejPropertiesNorth

I had the opportunity to co-host Bhavshala last year✨
A Retreat that changed my perception of Yoga & Bhakti. It’s much more than movement & breath work. I’m not religious, I don’t pray everyday.. but I understand that faith plays a great role in our lives. My faith, dharma and karma all are mother nature 🙏🌸 

I serve her however possible. & thats why sustainability was a part of this retreat. Helping people understand that you are one with god & one with nature. There are no two sides.. we are all one. It’s important to understand that now more than ever. Embrace oneness with us ☀️ 

If this feels like something you would want to be a part of, comment “Bhav” and I’ll send you the details😊

#bhavshala #yogaretreat #sustainableliving

items.caption_text:
Dog parenting today needs a mindset shift not another purchase.

Somewhere along the way, loving a dog became all about aesthetics. Fancy beds. Gourmet treats. Matching accessories. Spa packages. But your dog doesn’t measure love in products.

They measure it in your presence. In the way you play with them after a long day, long walks and shower your love and care for them.

And before buying another breed because it’s trending, maybe pause and ask: Are we choosing compassion… or chasing a social status?

Adopting isn’t “less than.” It’s love in its purest form. It’s choosing a life that’s already here, already waiting, already deserving.

Because responsible dog parenting isn’t about what you buy for them. It’s about the kind of human you choose to be for them.

Adopt, don't shop. That’s the real flex. 🐾

Share this with more and more people to generate awareness about the "right" dog parenting and let's create a kinder world for all. 

#consciouschoices #adoptdontshop #sustainability #dogparenting 

[adopt dont shop, voices for strays india, dog parenting, dog lovers india, indian dogs matter, dog brands, stop overconsumption, delhi dogs, adopt dogs, animal lovers, pet lovers, pet parenting, sustainable parenting, conscious parenting]

items.caption_text:
Why Upcycle? 

👚It takes thousands of litres of water to produce just one new garment. Fabrics are dyed with chemicals that pollute rivers.
👗 Synthetic materials shed microplastics into our oceans. And yet, so many beautiful clothes sit unused in our wardrobes.

Upcycling changes that ✅ 

♻️Upcycling:
• Reduces textile waste
• Conserves water and energy
• Lowers carbon emissions
• Slows down the demand for mass production
• Keeps memories alive in a new form

This isn’t just fashion — it’s responsibility.
It’s choosing intention over impulse, and Craft over convenience.

Each piece was thoughtfully created by @parijata_upcycle — a small business that puts time, care, and heart into making something uniquely yours. Not mass-produced. Not rushed. Just made right.

Because sustainability isn’t about buying more “eco” 
It’s about reimagining what we already have.

#Upcycling #SustainableFashion #SlowFashion #SupportSmallBusiness #upcycle

items.caption_text:
Delusion me jeeyein ya sachai me? 😵‍💫

I feel this country has become a political drama, jaha @officialrekhagupta ko AQI temperature lagta hai.. aur audience ko ye samjh nahi aa raha ki vo hasse ya roye! 

Aise me aap kya karoge? Sab ignore kar k ye hope karoge ki @wangchuksworld jaise log bacha lenge india aur environment ko jail ja k? Ya hum apni taraf se kuch kar sake uske bare me sochoge? 

Civic sense ki itne baatein chal rahi hain aj kal internet pe par fir bhi log sudarne ko teyar nahi. Ye delusion nahi toh kya hai? 

Apni individual responsibility jab hum citizens samjh jayenge na, tab shayad hum apni power bhi samjh jayenge. India ka democracy hona bhi delusion maan k haath mat khade karo! 

Do whatever you can in your capacity! PMO ko concerned letters bhejo, emails bhejo, bade polluting brands ko support karna band karo unki cheezein kharid kar. Jitna ho sake sustainable life jeene ka try karo. Kuch toh karo 🙏🏻

items.caption_text:
A day in Kashi🌸 

It was my first time visiting banaras and I surely understood the vibe of Kashi that everyone who has ever visited has told me! There’s a certain kind of belonging, the ghats, the temples, ganga maa🙏🏻 you can get lost there and still find yourself 💕 

Though I witnessed a lot of litter & garbage in residential areas, the ghats were being cleaned time to time. The air pollution can’t go unnoticed though 🫠 the AQI is bad, sky is mostly hazy, decade old boats running on diesel leave black smoke behind… I hope the government takes some measures to make Varanasi safer for our lungs 🙏🏻 @bjp4up 

Everyone talks about the energy of kashi, I hope everyone starts talking about the Air, water and soil of kashi too. 

Har har Mahadev 🙌
Jai maa gange 🙏🏻 

#kashi #banaras #ganga #varanasi #india

items.caption_text:
A devastating incident in Jhaniyar in Tirthan Valley, a remote village in Himachal Pradesh, needs urgent national attention.

A few days ago, a massive fire destroyed 16 out of 20 to 25 homes, which is almost the entire village. Jhaniyar is accessible only by a 40-minute uphill walk with no road connectivity, so the fire brigade could not reach in time. By the time the flames settled, families had lost their homes, essentials, documents and everything they owned.

We visited the site ourselves. Entire structures are now ash and twisted metal. People are living in temporary tents and neighbours’ homes while winter has already begun. This region receives three to four feet of snow, and temperatures drop sharply. Surviving the next few months will be extremely difficult.

The government has announced ₹8 lakh per family as relief, but only ₹50,000 has been disbursed so far. Apart from a few blankets and basic tents, very little support has reached the community. Right now people are cooking one shared langar-style meal a day, with no gas cylinders, no heating arrangements and no kitchen supplies.

Local NGOs and neighbouring villages are helping with whatever they can. But the gap is far larger than what small groups alone can cover.

I am sharing this because this situation needs wider coverage. It needs to reach policymakers, media, disaster management authorities and people who can help accelerate relief.

If you are a journalist, media platform, policy professional or someone with reach, please help amplify this. And if you would like to support directly, QR code is added in the comments. 
There is no personal benefit for me here. We are simply trying to ensure these families are not forgotten in the cold.

Visibility can change outcomes. #REPOST #SHARE

End to end on ground coverage, storyline and video editing by @anoop.grover 

#disaster #disastermanagement #disasterresponse

A devastating incident in Jhaniyar in Tirthan Valley, a remote village in Himachal Pradesh, needs urgent national attention.

A few days ago, a massive fire destroyed 16 out of 20 to 25 homes, which is almost the entire village. Jhaniyar is accessible only by a 40-minute uphill walk with no road connectivity, so the fire brigade could not reach in time. By the time the flames settled, families had lost their homes, essentials, documents and everything they owned.

We visited the site ourselves. Entire structures are now ash and twisted metal. People are living in temporary tents and neighbours’ homes while winter has already begun. This region receives three to four feet of snow, and temperatures drop sharply. Surviving the next few months will be extremely difficult.

The government has announced ₹8 lakh per family as relief, but only ₹50,000 has been disbursed so far. Apart from a few blankets and basic tents, very little support has reached the community. Right now people are cooking one shared langar-style meal a day, with no gas cylinders, no heating arrangements and no kitchen supplies.

Local NGOs and neighbouring villages are helping with whatever they can. But the gap is far larger than what small groups alone can cover.

I am sharing this because this situation needs wider coverage. It needs to reach policymakers, media, disaster management authorities and people who can help accelerate relief.

If you are a journalist, media platform, policy professional or someone with reach, please help amplify this. And if you would like to support directly, QR code is added in the comments. 
There is no personal benefit for me here. We are simply trying to ensure these families are not forgotten in the cold.

Visibility can change outcomes. #REPOST #SHARE

End to end on ground coverage, storyline and video editing by @anoop.grover 

#disaster #disastermanagement #disasterresponse

items.caption_text:
It’s incredibly convenient to tear open a chips or namkeen packet by just cutting the tip. However, we stop considering about where these small pieces of plastic end up. 🤷🏻‍♀️

Microplastics and nanoplastics can interfere with important biological processes in the human body 🧬 and can cause disruption of the endocrine, immune system; can have a negative impact on mobility, reproduction and development; and can cause carcinogenesis‼️

Stop throwing away packets like it doesn’t affect you, because microplastics will affects us, humans the most. ❌ 

#microplastics #plasticpollutes #plasticwaste #stoplittering #travelsustainably #livesustainably #india

items.caption_text:
We traveled where nature thrives 🌱 

In Sikkim, sustainability isn’t a trend — it’s a way of life. No plastic bottled water, organic farming, and the calmness of a state less populated and less polluted. While roaming around west and north sikkim, we could only think one thing- why is it that people travel abroad, when our own India is this beautiful !!! 
Sikkim has this unmatched beauty that at least I have never seen before. Hope it stays that way and everyone who travels to sikkim, travels sustainably. Honouring and preserving the nature. 💚 

#SustainableTravel #SikkimDiaries #sikkim #northeast

items.caption_text:
Give Plastic and Get Money? 💴 

Mumbai’s Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport (CSMIA) has installed Reverse Vending Machines (RVM) to encourage people to recycle plastic bottles. 

Basically, if you have an empty plastic bottle you can go and put it in the machine. The machine will then give you swiggy,  myntra coupons.

How cool is that? 

Personally I am not a fan of these coupons. Instead we should give points or cash coupons for donating bottles

Mumbai is following a scheme which is super successful in several european countries like Norway, Germany, Denmark. For example, Norway has had a bottle deposit scheme since 1902 and they continue to run successfully even to this day. In 2021, an average of 91.5 per cent of cans and 92.8 per cent of all purchased plastic bottles were returned in Norway

We are late to the party but these models of reverse vending machines can be a gam changer and must be scaled across India.  To begin with we must give incentives like points/cash to people.. but then when they get used to the system we must charge a small deposit amount for each plastic, glass, metal items so that everyone gets motivated to recycle their waste

What do you think of this recycling scheme? Interesting right?

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #sustainabilitytips #reels #reelsinstagram #mumbai #mumbaiairport

Eco Tips Shopping Edition 🛍️ 

If your day to day conversation revolves around grocery shopping, then you have officially become an adult 😄

3. Eat before going shopping, always helps 🥘

items.caption_text:
Tamil Nadu has become the first state in India to launch climate change missions in all of its 38 districts

What this means is that: every district will now be taking climate change seriously and will be developing and implementing climate change related initiatives to tackle issues specific to those districts

The best part is that, they have already setup a governance council headed by the chief minister and several climate change experts from across the globe to guide and lead this mission. 

Thats a very big step.. to be honest. Usually climate related initiatives are implemented at state level without a dedicated action plan and most times all these initiatives happen after an catastrophe .. like building some form of flood barriers after floods happen etc. So taking action pro-actively is a much needed change in our fight against climate change

So what actions can you expect from the expert council?

As a first step, they will  release data on carbon emissions for each of its departments 

They will also establish a platform (maybe a website or portal) to share all climate change information pertaining to Tamil Nadu State

I am excited to see this become successful and become as a model for all other states in India

What do you think?

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #sustainabilitytips #reels #reelsinstagram #climatechange #news

items.caption_text:
Menstrual Leave - Yes or No?

Nowadays there is a lot of debate happening around menstrual leave, is it needed or is it discriminatory? Lot of opinions being shared

But amidst this debate, we are forgetting one important aspect, that is about period poverty

Period Poverty is basically the inability of menstruating population to get access to  menstrual products and basic sanitation facilities. This form of poverty then deprives menstruators from basic opportunities like education, jobs etc.

For Instance in India, approximately 23 million girls drop out of school annually simply because of Period Poverty and many others miss classes on a monthly basis just because they don’t have menstrual products or toilets etc

This is sad, really! 

Government and various NGOs are trying to change this.. by making sanitary napkins and menstrual cups free for school students, going to rural areas and educating the population on hygiene etc

But it’s definitely not simple. Because there are scenarios where some NGO donated free disposable napkins to a village and at first, the menstruators used it. But after few days, they were sceptical. 

You know why?

Menstruators were throwing the used disposable napkin to the near by river, which was their source of drinking water.

So my view is that, we can’t look at such deep problems in a superficial way, we need to go deeper and also change our solutions to meet the needs of the people

What do you think? Whats your opinion on this topic? 

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #sustainabilitytips #reels #reelsinstagram #period #periodpoverty

items.caption_text:
Save Mangroves, to Save Mumbai

I repeat “Save Mangroves, to Save Mumbai”

Yes. Mangroves play an important role in protecting mumbai from coastal erosion, storm surges, and flooding. Basically, they act as a natural barrier, absorbing the energy of waves and reducing the impact of storms or sea level rise on the coast.  Somewhat like a sponge or shock absorber! So protecting them is very important 

If you don’t believe it, read about the real-life example of July 2005 Floods in Mumbai, when a record-breaking 944 millimeters of rain hit Mumbai for 24 hours. This flood destroyed thousands of properties, and over 1,000 people lost their lives to the floods. But a fishing village, Bhandup, escaped most of the damage. 

You know why? 

Mangrove forests surrounded this village and cushioned it from floods. 

Yes, it is that effective. 

However, the mangrove ecosystem in Mumbai is under threat from urbanization, waste dumping, and illegal land reclamation. Yes, government is now becoming more aware and have taken steps like setting up a “mangrove conservation cell” 

But it still needs our attention. Therefore, making this video.

So where to start?

As an individual, you can contribute by taking part in mangrove area cleanups and also planting more in nearby area. You can even attend workshops or read more about mangroves. 

There is also an app called “Mangroves App” which can help identify 22 mangrove species in Maharashtra, based on details such as the shape of the leaf or colour. This app is intended to increase interest in mangroves and their importance, and also raise awareness and conservation efforts.

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #sustainabilitytips #mangroves #reels #reelsinstagram

items.caption_text:
So France has banned single-use plates, cups, other cutleries for restaurants who serve customers coming to the store to eat food.

To put to context - There are roughly 30,000 fast-food outlets in France serving six billion meals a year. So with this ban an estimated 180,000 tons of waste is going to be reduced. 

You know what’s funny? 

In a way, we are actually going back to old methods of living.  I mean, like before every restaurant was using reusable cutlery’s, glasses and stuff to serve their dine-in customers, right?

But then with an aim to serve more customers, we opted for these throwaway measures.

The reason i am making this video is because In India, we are following the same path like them.. so I feel before its gets out of control, we must stop this throw away habit right here.

We have approximately 3 crores foodservice businesses in India excluding 55,000 hotels (FICCI). Out of this 70Lakh businesses are in the organised sector itself. Yes,  the impact we can create is huge. 

So please lets not take this for granted.. lets opt for reusable options 

However, We shouldn’t put a blanket ban. There must be some thinking, analysis given into how much water, energy use would increase because of the ban. So who is better off, who needs support etc must be studied in detail. Otherwise it will only shift problem from one place to another

Whats your view on this? Do let me know

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #reels #reelsinstagram #packagingban

items.caption_text:
Most of us are familiar with air, water, and land pollution, but did you know that light can also cause pollution ?

Hahaha don’t worry its something we can easily tackle

So light pollution is basically caused by inappropriate or excessive use of artificial light. So maybe when an outdoor lighting used at night is inefficient, or overly bright, poorly targeted and, in many cases, completely unnecessary. Also, even these office buildings litt up completely late in the night. I am sure most of ya’ll have seen that?

This light is considered to have some environmental consequences on humans, wildlife, and our climate. Studies have shown its impacting the circadian rhythm of both humans and wildlife. Like in the case of  pigeons, they were found to be confused and started waking up earlier than usual.. (then humans will complain.. birds chirping early is affecting their sleep quality haha) Now further studies are being done to see how this impacts their our world.

Fun fact: According to the 2016 groundbreaking “World Atlas of Artificial Night Sky Brightness,” 80 percent of the world’s population lives under such polluted skies. In a way, everyone of us is getting affected by this.

So what can we do?

Turn off all unnecessary lights to start with and maybe even ask our offices to turn off after everyone leaves

Hope you learnt something new

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainabilitytips #sustainability #reels #reelsinstagram

items.caption_text:
Your “Reusable Bottle” is still a good enough solution to tackle the issue of single use plastic. 

So please don’t panic reading some random research. 

The reason why I am calling this research random is because of the following:

Lack of Credibility: the company which has done this research is not a scientific institution nor has this research been peer-reviewed or published in a credible journal. Its just an article published in the website of a private company founded in the year 2020 , with 3-4 employees (as per their website and LinkedIn) 

Incomplete Methodology: The company has not published any data on how they selected the sample.. what kind of criteria they set for selecting. For example, if they selected sample like a rusted bottle, old bottle or even bottle with normal use.

So we can’t really come to a conclusion that reusable bottles are bad or has more bacteria compared to toilet seat 

Key Takeaway from the Study:

Yes. We must clean our bottles regularly, otherwise they can accumulate a lot of bacteria - just like any other vessel in your household. So just chill and wash your bottles at least once a day! 😄

Hope this post solves the confusion

#sustainability #thesustainabilityguy #reels #reelsinstagram #ecotips #reusablebottles

items.caption_text:
Wondered what happens to your all-natural food/organic waste after you throw it in the dustbin? 

Hmm.. yeah bro - if it’s thrown without segregation, then all of the waste ends up in landfill. Once it reaches here, then it releases a gas called methane – a harmful greenhouse gas that is 25 times more potent than carbon dioxide

So we should stop this habit ok? 

You know something..most times we throw our organic waste in the same bin and don’t feel guilty about it because in our minds we feel these are natural items and there is no reason to associate anything bad with this 

So that’s why governments keep telling us, separate wet waste and dry waste. So when it is separated then, the wet waste goes to the community composter which is now getting installed in most municipalities (If there is no community composter, then you never know)

But the best solution out there is to do the composting ourselves - if you want to learn about it then do follow @wormrani she has already uploaded a lot of videos on this.

Hope this video helps some of you in taking that decision. 

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainabilitytips #sustainability #reels #reelsinstagram #composting #reducereuserecycle

items.caption_text:
Is it Safe to Reuse Plastic Water Bottles?

No, its not recommended 

Most water bottles even has an image asking users to throw the bottle in the bin after single use.

But why?

1. Chemical Leaching: This is when chemicals from the plastic mix with whatever liquid you put inside.. due to sunlight hitting the bottle or wear and tear causing cracks in the bottle 
2. Bacteria Growth: This can happen quickly from the ordinary use.  Even unfinished beverages left outside is seen to have bacteria growth

With these factores in mind, I am sure you are now thinking of going for a reusable bottle 😉 

Source: WebMD 

#sustainability #thesustainabilityguy #reels #reelsinstagram #reducereuserecycle

items.caption_text:
Do you carry your reusable cups when you go to grab your favourite coffee from starbucks?

You carry right? Please tell yes

If you don’t.. then don’t worry

Starbucks actually has got no problem if you carry your cups. Infact they even give you 10 rupee discount if you bring your cup (I asked the malleswaram and brigade road branch in Bengaluru and they confirmed this) 😄

Ok? 

Just because you are reading this, let me also tell you a dirty secret 😄

These disposable coffee cups are not really recyclable. Even though it looks like or feels like paper, these  cups have a  plastic lining in them which makes it hard to recycle. Most brands including starbucks are trying for decades now to make coffee cups recyclable but have not been successful (don’t know if they really want to or not)

But anyways, carrying your reusable cup is the best solution. You get your favourite coffee and also get to reduce your impact

Hope you learnt something new. If you did, like share maaadi 😄

#sustainabilitytips #sustainability #thesustainabilityguy #reels #reelsinstagram #reduceplasticwaste

items.caption_text:
Hyderabad is famous for its biryani, movies and of course all its tourist attractions. 

right? 

But in the future, they will also be famous for developing  India’s first 100 per cent green government building. 

Yes. The State Renewable Energy Development Corporation Limited (TSREDCO) is building a new office in Hyderabad and it is going to be eco friendly 

Like it it will have a Grid- Interactive Net Zero Energy structure spread over 2,591 square metres with a roof garden, wind tower, Building Integrated Photovoltaics (BVIP), recycled timber vertical slabs and measures to control urban flooding in and around the building.  The design also takes into consideration many natural elements so that use of light and ACs is highly reduced 

You know why this is unique ? 

Usually only private sector offices adopt all these green building designs but in this case it’s government. So there are high chances that, once this becomes operational, more such environmental-friendly buildings will come up in large numbers across the country

What do you think?

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #ecofriendlybuildings #greenbuildings #sustainabilitytips #reels #reelsinstagram

items.caption_text:
Knock Knock.. 

Whose there?

Your food delivery person! :D

I am sure, everyone of you gets super excited when the food delivery person comes to your doors. If you don’t what even bro :D

But there is one thing which is often not spoken about when it comes to food delivery -that’s the impact on their environment. 

As per the survey conducted in 2018, about 22,000 tonnes of plastic waste was generated by online delivery apps such as Zomato, Swiggy, etc. majorly due to its plastic cutlery. Imagine the overall impact, if we calculate the waste that is being generated  from containers, bowls, cups, lids, foil, sachets, delivery packets, etc.

If you want to do the calculation: 35-40million orders are delivered per month by food delivery companies combined in India

Hugeeeeeee! 

So the least we can do is to reduce online ordering - maybe when we are super tired, then yeah go for it. But once it’s delivered, we must rinse the utensils and store all of the dry waste. Then when after few months, when we collect some good quantity of waste,  we can give it to a recycling facility near by. 

Easy right?

Also, brands must try to change their business model and use reusable containers. Yes, that’s also proven and possible. Startups in the USA like Go Box and DeliverZero for example are using reusable food containers for their food deliveries

What do you think?

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainabilitytips #sustainability #reels #reelsinstagram #endplasticpollution #onlinedelivery

items.caption_text:
India makes USB Type-C charging must for device makers from March 2025

Yes. This is happening (at least as per latest information)

By March 2025, the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIF) will be facilitating the introduction of this universal charger.

This initiative is being implemented with an aim of reducing e-waste. 

Yes. E-wastes are becoming a major environmental concern due to the presence of toxic substances such as lead, mercury and cadmium that can contaminate soil and water sources. In 2020-2021, India generated approximately 3.4 lakh tonnes of e-waste. 

Yes. I know, Chargers produces just a small % of e-wastes but what this law will eventually do is, push the responsibility towards the manufacturers

BTW this is not new, Europe has already brought a similar law.. from 2024, all the portable electronic devices  there will have type C charger.

Anyways what do you think? If you are an iPhone user, then I feel you must be happy. No more running around for chargers 

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainabilitytips #sustainability #reels #reelsinstagram

items.caption_text:
Clothes made from plastic bottles - cool or not?

Ill let you take that guess 😉 

In this video I have tried to highlight the “not so cool” side of the product which no one talks about 

Yes. Whenever clothes are made of plastic bottle.. and when it gets washed….it releases something called as microplastics.. these microplastic like the name suggests are super small in size, so most times we don’t even see its existence

Thats the scary part as well. Because these microplastics are so small, it enters the water bodies like rivers, oceans without any problem

👉 Key takeaways: 

Plastic will still be plastic, be it as a bottle or made in the shape of good looking clothes

Whenever brands come out with some “new” solution to the sustainability problem.. always pause and think about it. If its really sustainable. Because from my experience working in this field for over 5 years, what I have seen and also realised is that - everything related to sustainability has mostly been about getting media attention.. if a can be advertised and brands can benefit from it, then that becomes a solution. 

What do you think?

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #reels #reelsinstagram #plasticpollution #endplasticwaste

items.caption_text:
“You know in the future, we will most likely be charged for using electric devices depending on the time of the day

You don’t believe me?

Hahaha it is already a reality in many countries including some parts of India for commercial clients like factories

So there is something called as  time of day (TOD) tariff  which applies variable charges depending on what time of day electricity is used. For example, the price of consuming 1 unit of power will be 10 INR maybe during peak hours (6 a.m. to 10 a.m and 6 p.m. to 9 p.m) compared to say 6 INR during non-peak hours

So this way we can plan our day better and mostly use big equipments like washing machine, grinders during non-peak hours to save on our electricity bills (per unit can save a lot of paisaa 😄) 

Why is the government doing this?

To reduce “peak demand” - so this is a bit technical, I will give a simple example if want to learn about it

During hot summer days, our energy demand for electricity is highest, so keeping this demand in mind, governments build electricity generation plants.. but during say winter months this demand is low. So now what about the electricity generation plants? Will they stay idle? So in order to avoid such unnecessary construction they implement schemes like this to manage the supply and demand 

Same is the case on a day to day basis, during peak hours its highest and then it falls down.. so to manage the power and grid health across the day, such schemes are implemented 

Hope this video is helpful

Also, I am sorry many people checked in on me to see how I am doing and why I am not posting videos. Thank you for that. I am doing good and will slowly start posting some exciting content soon❤️

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #reels #reelsinstagram #reducelectricity

items.caption_text:
Everyone knows plastic cups are bad when it comes to its environmental impact..

So most of us now go with paper cups as an alternative to plastic

But do you know even that is not perfect

You know why ?  Disposable paper cups are moslyy made of around 90 to 95% paper, but an additional 5% is made up of a thin plastic coating of polyethelene

This plastic coating is added to make the paper cup waterproof, so that it retains heat and allows it to maintain its shape

and the sad part is when the plastic coating  gets exposed to hot liquids, then it starts to break down, and releases microplastics into your drink

Yes there are other type of paper cups which are made of food grade wax too, but how do we distinguish between the two? Manufacturers also use cheap wax sometimes to reduce their costs, which could then have health impacts

Sad right?

I understand its hard to completely do away with disposable cups, but one can seriously reduce the usage by carrying a reusable bottle everywhere you go. In offices, keep a glass or mug to sip your favourite coffee everytime

Hope this helps

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #sustainabilitytips #reelsinstagram #reels

items.caption_text:
Sprite… clear hai?

Coca Cola has announced they are going to change the colour of their flagship Sprite brand from Green to White, to improve the quality of recycling

Ok let me explain how this works: 

See India has one of the highest PET (the type of plastic used to make plastic bottles) recycling rates - nearly 85% of all PET bottles are recycled (Source: TERI, NCL) but much of this recycling (nearly 70%) is recycled by the fibre industry to make clothes, carpets etc. 

Now this is what coca cola is trying to change.. they want to make bottles from recycled materials.  So by changing the colour of their plastic bottles to white, they are claiming they can get good quality plastic pellets, which can be then used to make bottles instead of clothes etc 

Yes, this is true but will it make a big difference to coca cola’s exceptional performance being the world’s top most plastic polluter for 4-5 years in a row?

Fun Fact:  They sell nearly 100 billion plastic bottles a year 😄

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #reels #reelsinstagram #sprite #endplasticpollution #cocacola

items.caption_text:
What if someone tells you, you have just 6 years 255 days left to live? How would you react? 

Maybe go to the doctor for solutions right?

What if the doctor then gives you hope and asks you to do certain activities to live more? 

Most of us would do it, right

That’s the situation with climate change.. there is a clock being installed across the globe to demonstrate how quickly our planet is approaching 1.5 °C of global warming, given current emissions trends and scientific evidences. Once the clock reaches this point, then there will be lot of irreversible impacts across the world.. like floods, heatwaves, droughts affecting food, energy security etc. 

BTW the clock is updated every year to reflect the latest global CO2 emissions trend and rate of climate warming. 

Yeah, basically doctor telling us the date and then asking us to take the necessary precautions to tackle climate change. 

What do you think? Did you know of the climate clock? Will this even be taken seriously? Let me know in the comments

#sustainability #thesustainabilityguy #reels #reelsinstagram #climateclock

items.caption_text:
What do you normally use fishing nets for?

To catch a fish right?

Now imagine if that same net and the techniques is used to catch plastic which is in the water before it enters the sea? Cool right? 

Yeah - that’s what Mumbai is doing righy now. 

Few NGOs, think-thanks like Bombay61 Studio (B61), Ministry of Mumbai’s Magic (MMM), research entity Tapestry along with  the “Koli community” came together, designed this entire initiative and conducted a pilot initiative on the banks of versova creek and it was a success. 

They collected 500kg of plastic in just 3 days.  Now they are hoping government takes notice of this and helps them scale it across Mumbai. But I also hope waste is reduced at source, because this solution will  only reduce waste entering the sea, but waste will still be generated. So for that, we have to tackle the root problem

P.S. I love this initiative because its entirely designed by taking into consideration of the local community. I feel this is important because, most times we think western solutions, machines are the “only” way to go ahead. But its not true. We have to listen to the locals, understand their perspective  about the place and challenges, and then use modern technology wherever necessary. 

Hope you like it 

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainabilitytips #sustainability #reelsinstagram #reels #mumbai

items.caption_text:
India is leading the world in the aviation sector when it comes to gender diversity 

According to the International Society of Women Airline Pilots, 12.4% of all pilots in India are women. This is a good number( For comparison, major markets like US and UK have 4-5% itself) 

But how did India reach this level ?

Mainly good policies by airlines: For example, airlines offer a flexible work environment  to women crew members and pilots. Not just that some airlines also offer temperory on ground roles to pregnant women to support them during materinity. This is a big step considering how many women leave work after pregnancy in India

Another major reason behind India’s success story in employing more women is the outreach programs. These programs include awareness regarding job opportunities in the industry. So attending these events allowed women to think about this career option and then pursue it

Because of all this, a level of trust and comfort was built within women. Even their family members (who are often times the barrier) were ok with them pursuing it

But I hope it reflects in  other sectors too, because currently our female labour force participation rates are very low (20-25% compared to 45-50% averages)

But this is definitely a good achievement, what do you think?

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #genderdiversity #female #pilots #reels #reelsinstagram

items.caption_text:
Oil and gas industries have 636 representatives at COP 27 (climate conference) – a rise of more than 25% on previous year. 

You know what? This number is only increasing year on year and that is the main problem. Because with every year, their influence on the outcome, policies of climate summits is getting skewed to meet “their” needs, rather than needs of the developing countries or countries getting affected the most due to climate change

I also feel, If a very important and neutral conference like COP becomes a meet and greet event for fossil fuel companies, then its hard to get an optimistic conclusion right?  If not COP, where do we go? 

So I feel, we must not allow this trend to go unnoticed and ask united nations, where they are heading towards. 

What do you think?

#sustainability #thesustainabilityguy #reels #reelsinstagram #ACECC #ACEPartner and #AllEyesOn27 #cop27

items.caption_text:
Good News Alert

Bengaluru airport T2 is getting inaugurated tomorrow. You know what’s so special? The entire terminal is designed with sustainability factors in mind. The theme of the new terminal is that of a “garden” - so there will be lot of plants, gardens, ponds and even a 3 storey forest inside the terminal. The concept aims to give passengers a natural feel of a garden as they pass through every terminal touchpoint

There will also be minimum reliance on artificial illumination, treated water used for the landscape indoor gardens, and this also means that - less dependence on mechanical ACs

Why this is a good step?

Well its true aviation industry is not a big contributor to India’s overall emissions yet (just 1-3%) at the moment and airports contribute to less than 10% of this. So yeah the role of airports is kinda minimal compared to emissions of the airlines and other transport related emissions related to aviation section (90% emissions) 

But that doesn’t mean airports shouldn’t take climate action. Also, this airport can also do what changi airport has done to singapore - inspire a lot of young people people about sustainability and technology etc and become a beacon of hope for the city. This is very much needed particularly in a city like bengaluru.

BTW, this design is also a tribute to revive bengaluru’s identity as the Garden City of India (YES before the Silicon Valley Tag). I really hope this design and identity gets reflected in all the developmental projects that happens here

What’s your view about Bengaluru KIA T2 ?

raw.caption:
Good News Alert

Bengaluru airport T2 is getting inaugurated tomorrow. You know what’s so special? The entire terminal is designed with sustainability factors in mind. The theme of the new terminal is that of a “garden” - so there will be lot of plants, gardens, ponds and even a 3 storey forest inside the terminal. The concept aims to give passengers a natural feel of a garden as they pass through every terminal touchpoint

There will also be minimum reliance on artificial illumination, treated water used for the landscape indoor gardens, and this also means that - less dependence on mechanical ACs

Why this is a good step?

Well its true aviation industry is not a big contributor to India’s overall emissions yet (just 1-3%) at the moment and airports contribute to less than 10% of this. So yeah the role of airports is kinda minimal compared to emissions of the airlines and other transport related emissions related to aviation section (90% emissions) 

But that doesn’t mean airports shouldn’t take climate action. Also, this airport can also do what changi airport has done to singapore - inspire a lot of young people people about sustainability and technology etc and become a beacon of hope for the city. This is very much needed particularly in a city like bengaluru.

BTW, this design is also a tribute to revive bengaluru’s identity as the Garden City of India (YES before the Silicon Valley Tag). I really hope this design and identity gets reflected in all the developmental projects that happens here

What’s your view about Bengaluru KIA T2 ?

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #thesustainabilityguy #reels #reelsinstagram #bengaluru #bengaluruairport

items.caption_text:
We all love to buy clothes and often times we get confused about which one to buy, right?

We think, clothes that cost lower means its a good deal and vice versa, we think higher price clothes are not affordable. That’s not actually true

Let me explain:

Lets say you are confused between 2 plain tshirts, one costs 500 and other costs 1000. If you are worried about the budget, you would go for the 500 one, thinking its a good deal.  But its not always the case 

So in such situations, its always good to estimate how many times you expect to wear the tshirt before buying it

Scenario 1: Black tshirt which you expect to wear 5 times or which will last you 5 times

Cost (Black) = 500
Expected to use (Black) = 10 times
Cost per use =500/10= 100 rupees per use

Scenario 2: white tshirt which you expect to wear 20 times or which will last you for 20 times

Cost (White) = 1000
Expected to use (White) = 20
Cost per use = 1000/20 = 50 rupees per use

So this way in reality scenario 2 is cheaper for you per use. 

Got it?

#thesustainabilityguy #reelsinstagram #reels #sustainabilitytips #sustainability #reelsindia

China is going all-in on Panda’s, I mean Panda shaper Solar Power Plants

So china has developed a 250-acre solar farm in the shape of a panda. The effort is partially a PR campaign to attract chinese youth towards clean energy and also to showcase its might to the global audience that they are leading the world in renewable energy investment.

items.caption_text:
We can’t deny - we are all facing huge impacts (floods, heatwaves, droughts etc) due to climate change 

Almost everyday there is a media headline of some place within India or outside in chaos - all of this is bad, particularly for developing countries.

Why? See our per capita is low, we have so many millions of people living on daily wages and also in poverty. So during extreme weather events, they are the first in line to face the brunt (Check videos of Pakistan, what happened to their people) 

But the sad part is that - developing countries have not caused this problem and expecting them to go “all-in” on environment when there is so more important social complexities, is not fair in any ways. 

Developed countries, like the U.S and those in the European Union, are responsible for most of the heat-trapping emissions. So they must compensate poorer ones for the “loss and damage” caused by climate change.

Next week at the COP27 summit, this is going to be a big agenda point (I’ll update you guys about it) 

Can India and other developing countries get them to pay for it? Lets wait and watch

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #sustainabilitytips #reels #reelsinstagram

items.caption_text:
“Ek letter ki kheemat tum kya janoge ramesh babu?”

Letter?

Yes. Letter is a very powerful tool which can be used to raise your concerns, particularly if the company which you are working is not doing anything for causes like climate change. You can simply write an open letter collectively among your peers (ofcourse in a polite tone) stating why causes like climate change must be supported by the company and why its an important issue for you as an individual/group. 

But what actions can you demand on an immediate basis?

Well, In my current company - we demanded actions ranging from banning single-use plastic, installing waste segregation bins, turning off lights after office hours, changes to HVAC systems, training and incentives to help people make better environmental choices. 

But then there are many strategic requests you can make as well - like reducing emissions to net zero and also first calculating how much of current business that comes from fossil fuel and high emitting companies, then slowly arriving at a consensus to reduce business relationships with such clients. 

What do you think? Have you had such conversations at your workplace? If not, what is stopping you? Do let me know in the comments

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #sustainabilitytips #reels #reelsinstagram #letter

items.caption_text:
We’ve all been there before: Just last week I was checking for a nice shoes online and the discounts were crazy. The minute I saw the discounts on the product. I put it in my cart. No questions asked. 

Luckily I don’t keep my card linked to my account and  the card which I use for online transaction, I keep in my cupboard drawer and I was lazy to complete the full transaction. Anyways I planned to buy this shoes later. 

After sometime my mind changed - I started asking myself do I really need it, is it better to buy something else etc. Unintentionally I was following the 24 hours rule- under this rule, you basically the delay the completion of the transaction, so if you like a product, you put it in your cart and wait for 24 hours and then take a call if you really want or not (ofcourse I didn’t buy it yet - still it irks me every now and then). So I thought let me make a reel only on this topic 😄

If you go through such mad implulsive reactions, lol then you are not alone.  24 hours rule - works okishly for me, sometimes the cravings takes over.

What methods do you follow to stop this? 

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #reels #reelsinstagram #impulseshopping #impulsebuy #business

items.caption_text:
Tree planting is a nobel act, no doubt. But we must also give equal importance to preserving the existing trees

Why am I talking about this?

Well, if you clearly observe, thesedays we see a lot of trees stuck or nailed with advertisement posters of big companies - maybe because this is like free advertisement and no one really cares about the legality

But this is really bad for the trees, as it creates holes that give access to termites and insects.  Often times, advertisement companies also inject the trees with mercury and sulphuric acid. This way slowly the tree becomes weak. Once a tree becomes weak, then you know right - companies wait like a hawk to cut it

Sad

But do you know doing all this illegal (like most things) and you can actually file a complaint with the local municipal body. Otherwise, you can also click pictures and ring those companies and request them to remove it or you can also hold them responsible on social media - that also works

What do you think? Have you seen such trees in your local viscinity? 

Lets see if we can make a difference 

Source: NDTV Swachh India 

#thesustainabilityguy #sustainability #reelsinstagram #reels #trees

items.caption_text:
🔴 We’ve normalised wearing polyester so much… we don’t even question it anymore.

But here’s the truth polyester is basically plastic. And when you wear it daily, your skin is in constant contact with synthetic fibers that don’t breathe, trap heat, and can even release microplastics.

Over time, this can lead to skin irritation, allergies, excessive sweating, and discomfort. And when your skin can’t breathe, it affects more than just how you feel it impacts your overall health.

What’s more concerning is that every wash releases microplastics into water systems, which eventually come back to us through food, water, and air.

This isn’t about completely eliminating it overnight, but becoming more aware of what we wear daily. Choosing natural fabrics like cotton, linen, or hemp can make a real difference for your body and the planet.

Because what you wear shouldn’t silently harm you or the planet. Save this and share with your fam. 🌿

#SustainableFashion #SayNoToPolyester #ConsciousLiving #SlowFashion

[polyster is plastic, stop wearing synthetic fabrics, what to wear for better health, fast fashion problem, plastic waste, plastic is not for skin, natural alternatives]

items.caption_text:
For the longest time, periods felt like something we just had to “deal with” - discomfort, waste, and all. But the moment I switched to "period underwear" from @my.mahina I realised it doesn’t have to be that way.

No plastic. No constant buying. No irritation.
Just comfort, ease, and a sense of being more in tune with my body.

What we often don’t realise is that a single person can generate thousands of pads’ worth of waste in a lifetime. And most of it never really breaks down. It just sits there… long after we’re done with it.

Switching isn’t just about sustainability - it’s about choosing something better for yourself too. Something gentler, simpler, and more mindful.

It might feel like a small change, but it’s one that impacts both your body and the planet in the long run.

Sometimes, the most personal choices… create the biggest impact. 🌿

#SustainableLiving #PeriodCare #EcoFriendly #ConsciousChoices #ZeroWaste

items.caption_text:
Childhood was never meant to be cluttered. It was meant to be imagined, created, and felt.

Somewhere along the way, we started believing that "more toys = better parenting".
More options, more excitement, more happiness.

But the truth is - excess doesn’t build engagement, it shortens it!

When toys are endless, attention becomes limited. When everything is available, nothing feels special.

What if we paused and asked:
❓Are we buying for our child’s growth… or for momentary happiness?

Because real play doesn’t come from quantity.
It comes from "open-ended experiences" - toys that allow children to think, create, and explore at their own pace.

Choosing mindful toys isn’t about giving less. It’s about giving better things that last longer, engage deeper, and don’t end up as waste or harm the planet.

If you’re looking to shift towards more conscious choices, here are a few options that truly focus on intentional play:

🌱 @Todds.in – www.toddslife.com
🌱@Gubbacchi_kids – gubbachhi.com
🌱 @Neemtoys – neemtoys.in

This isn’t about being a perfect parent. It’s about being an aware one. Because the kind of play we choose today shapes not just our children…but the world they grow up in.

Save this. And share it with parents who are trying to raise with intention 🌍

#mindfulparenting #ConsciousChoices #SustainableLiving 

[Mindful toys, Sustainable Toys, EcoFriendly Kids, Open ended play, Child Development, naturalToys , Reduce Consumption, Green Future, Indian Art , Neem wood toys, play Matters, Planet Protection , Quality Over Quantity, Outdoor Play, Toddler Toys, green toys, non toxic toys]

items.caption_text:
We’ve been sold a very strange idea of “luxury.”
That convenience = class. That using disposables at every gathering somehow feels premium.

Disposable plates at functions. Mini plastic water bottles handed out like it’s normal.
But if we really think about it… is this uplift, or just waste wrapped as status?

Because the reality is most of this doesn’t get recycled. Not efficiently, not at scale. It ends up in landfills, in our water systems, and eventually back into our lives in ways we don’t even see.
True luxury isn’t in using something once and throwing it away. It’s in creating systems that last. In choosing reusables. In being mindful of the impact we leave behind.

Maybe it’s time we unlearn what we’ve been calling “premium” and redefine it as something that actually respects the planet.

[ConsciousLiving , disposables vs reusables, say no to disposables, Sustainable Choices say no To Plastic, Mindful Living, Waste Free, conscious planet, conscious choices, planet friendly]

#climateaction #disposableculture #consciousliving #choosereusable #ecofriendlyliving

items.caption_text:
🔴 I was shocked to see how easily these chemicals are added to our skincare and beauty products without really thinking about what they’re doing to our health over time.

It’s time to pause, question, and make a conscious switch.

Here are some better alternatives you can start with:

🌿 Homemade skincare
Simple, effective, and chemical-free. Ingredients like haldi, besan, aloe vera, rose water, and coconut oil can do wonders when used right. You know exactly what you’re putting on your skin no hidden toxins. Check my youtube video for details. Link in Bio 

🌱 Vegan & cruelty-free products
Choose brands that are transparent about their ingredients and don’t test on animals. Look for products made with plant-based, natural ingredients that are gentle on your skin and kinder to the planet.

💚 Because skincare isn’t just about looking good it’s about feeling safe in what you use every single day.

Start small: read your labels, question your products, and make one conscious switch today.
Because what you put on your skin matters just as much as what you put in your body.

👇 Tag brands which fall under the category of clean, vegan and cruelty free products and let's generate awareness!

items.caption_text:
Every child learns differently. Some take a little longer, some just need the right support, and some simply need to be seen and understood. 

But when we rush learning, compare journeys, or overlook these differences - we don’t just create a learning gap, we create a confidence gap. And that gap doesn’t just impact education, it shapes the future of an entire generation. 

Because education isn’t just a growth parameter - it’s the foundation of a sustainable, inclusive future. A future where every child gets the chance to grow at their own pace, without being left behind. 

It’s heartening to see efforts that are working towards this change. @Pgshiksha is now impacting 1cr children and more, helping bridge this gap and create equal learning opportunities. 

Because when we choose to invest in every child, we’re choosing a better tomorrow. 

P&G Supports education. Choose P&G. 

#EraseTheLearningGap #PGshiksha #AbsabjayendgeSchool #educationindia #climateaction

items.caption_text:
🔴 Before you throw away that old t-shirt or worn-out jeans, pause for a second. Most of what we call “waste” can actually be "repaired, reworn, or recycled".

Textile waste is one of the biggest contributors to landfills today, but small choices like extending the life of your clothes can make a real difference. And when something truly can’t be used anymore, that’s where @Gudswap comes in 🌱

They help "donate, upcycle, and recycle" clothes from everyday wear (yes, even undergarments) to soft toys, home linens, and fabric scraps.

If you’re in Bengaluru, you can either "drop off or send your items" to their textile recovery facility (Akkayamma Nilaya, Vijinapura Extension, Tues–Sun, 11 AM–5 PM), or even organise a "collection drive in your community, school, or office" by reaching out at [hello@gudswap.com]

Because responsible fashion isn’t just about buying better it’s also about "disposing better". ♻️

#TextileWaste #SustainableLiving #RecycleClothes #Upcycle #ConsciousLiving #CircularFashion

[ how to recycle responsibly in bengaluru, textile upcycle in bengaluru, how to donate responsible, old clothes upcyle, circular economy, textile recycle facility in bengaluru, fast fashion problem, upcyle old clothes]

#TextileWaste #SustainableLiving #RecycleClothes #Upcycle #ConsciousLiving #CircularFashion

items.caption_text:
🔴 Ep: 6 Commercialisation of Childhood Every small choice we make becomes a reflection of the values we pass on. The air they breathe, the spaces they grow in, the habits they witness… it all shapes their future more than we realize.

If we truly care about our children, then creating a cleaner, kinder, and more mindful planet isn’t optional it’s our responsibility.

@Diamirzaofficial has consistently celebrated her child’s birthdays with a zero-waste approach focusing on mindful decor, reusable elements, and experiences over excess. Similarly, @SonikaBhasin has been consciously hosting eco-friendly celebrations for her kid, proving that sustainability can be joyful, aesthetic, and deeply meaningful.

This is what real care looks like. Because parenting isn’t just about raising a child it’s about shaping the world they will grow up in. The values we practice become their normal.

And if we truly love them, then protecting their future means protecting this planet through our everyday choices, our celebrations, and the example we set. A better world doesn’t happen later.
It begins with how we choose to show up right from day one 🌱

#mindfulparenting #sustainableliving #consciouschoices

items.caption_text:
Sometimes it gets overwhelming…in a world like this
Seeing plastic everywhere, declining air quality, health slowly being impacted, and nature constantly being polluted it can feel like too much.

Especially in a fast-paced city like Mumbai, everything feels even more intense.

But this time, I chose something different.
Instead of getting anxious… I chose to stay calm 

Because constant worry doesn’t heal the planet conscious action does.

So this time, I focused on the good. The small mindful choices, the better alternatives, the people who care, the little efforts that are actually making a difference.

Maybe we can’t fix everything overnight…But we can choose how we respond.

Less panic.More intention, awareness, gratitude 🤍

Because healing the planet also begins with a calm and grounded mind 🌍

#MindfulLiving #SustainableChoices #ChooseCalm #MumbaiDiaries

[sustainable travels, mindful travel, sustainable living, conscious consumption, climate action, better planet, better India]

items.caption_text:
This isn’t art. This isn’t creativity. This is cruelty and it cost a life. An innocent elephant was painted, exploited, and used as a prop for human entertainment… and it led to her death. Let that sink in.

And this is not an isolated incident. Millions of animals suffer and die every single day for fashion, entertainment, tourism, and profit. Behind every “aesthetic” photo or viral video, there is often fear, pain, and silent suffering we choose to ignore.

We have normalised this. We scroll past it. We even applaud it. It’s time to stop.

Animals are not objects. They are living beings who feel, who trust, who suffer. They deserve dignity, not decoration. Freedom, not control.

If our idea of beauty or success comes at the cost of a life, then it’s not beauty- it’s brutality.

Be conscious of what you support. Because every view, every like, every purchase is a vote.

Choose compassion. Always. 🌿🐘💔
#pinkelephant #animalexploitation #animalcruelty #saveanimals 

[ pink elephant, jaipur elephant news, animal exploitation, animal cruelty, wildlife protection, animal rights, jaipur elephant shoot, elephant cruelty in tourism, elephant exploitation in india, animal ethics, better india, kinder india]

items.caption_text:
This is a conversation I wish more of us had with ourselves. Not just about what we’re buying, but why we’re buying it and what it truly costs beyond the price tag.

Fashion has normalised inflated pricing and constant discounts so much that we rarely stop to question what we’re actually paying for. But transparency in pricing is where real change begins. When we understand the true cost of our clothes, we automatically become more mindful of our choices.

That’s why initiatives like @virgio.official ’s Eco-nic Fair feel like a step in the right direction. For 5 days (27th–31st March), they’re removing retail markups and offering clothes at their actual cost of production making pricing more honest and conscious.

Because the shift isn’t just about buying less, it’s about buying better, with awareness.

#TransparentFashion #Consciousfashion #SustainableFashion #Buyless #mindfulfashion
[fast fashion need to stop, conscious fashion india, transparent pricing in fashion, virgio india, sustainable fashion, shop less]

items.caption_text:
For years, we’ve focused on awareness talking about pollution, climate change, and sustainability. But the scale of the environmental crisis today demands something more than conversations. It demands solutions that work in the real world.

India now needs innovators, startups, institutions, and change makers who are already building technologies and systems that can create measurable environmental impact at scale. The future of a cleaner country will depend on solutions that are tested, verified, and capable of transforming how we address air, water, and plastic pollution.

That’s exactly what the Zero Prize challenge is aiming to support - real, impact-driven environmental solutions that can help move India toward a pollution-free future.

The challenge launches on March 15, 2026 and applications will remain open until August 2026, giving innovators across the country an opportunity to step forward with solutions that can make a real difference.

Because the time for only discussing the problem is over.
Now it’s time to build and scale the solutions. From pollution to pollution-free India.

#ZeroPrizeIndia #ZeroPollutionIndia #CleanIndiaChallenge
#ResultsNotPromises #InnovationForImpact #SPGIn

[pollution free india, policies for a pollution free india, how to make india pollution free, oppurtunity for green innovators, solution for pollution, AQI problem in india, AQI issue]

items.caption_text:
Dog parenting today needs a mindset shift not another purchase.

Somewhere along the way, loving a dog became all about aesthetics. Fancy beds. Gourmet treats. Matching accessories. Spa packages. But your dog doesn’t measure love in products.

They measure it in your presence. In the way you play with them after a long day, long walks and shower your love and care for them.

And before buying another breed because it’s trending, maybe pause and ask: Are we choosing compassion… or chasing a social status?

Adopting isn’t “less than.” It’s love in its purest form. It’s choosing a life that’s already here, already waiting, already deserving.

Because responsible dog parenting isn’t about what you buy for them. It’s about the kind of human you choose to be for them.

Adopt, don't shop. That’s the real flex. 🐾

Share this with more and more people to generate awareness about the "right" dog parenting and let's create a kinder world for all. 

#consciouschoices #adoptdontshop #sustainability #dogparenting 

[adopt dont shop, voices for strays india, dog parenting, dog lovers india, indian dogs matter, dog brands, stop overconsumption, delhi dogs, adopt dogs, animal lovers, pet lovers, pet parenting, sustainable parenting, conscious parenting]
